description: Explain how text features contribute to meaning in an informational text; identify the text structure of sequence.
description_plain: Your child can explain how parts like headings and pictures help understand an informational text and can recognize when the text tells events in order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe the basic structure of a literary text (e.g., narrative, drama, and poem).
description_plain: Your child can describe the basic parts of different stories, like stories that are told as narratives, plays, or poems.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate and critique how an author uses words, phrases, and text structures to craft text.
description_plain: Your child can talk about how an author uses words and text layout to make the writing clear and interesting.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify and explain an author's purpose in a text, including what the author wants to answer, explain, or describe.
description_plain: Your child can figure out why an author wrote a text and what the author wants to explain or answer.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate and critique how an author's perspective and purpose shape style and meaning within and across informational texts.
description_plain: Your child understands how an author's viewpoint and reason for writing affect the style and meaning of informational texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify different characters' perspectives in a literary text.
description_plain: Your child can tell how different characters see things in a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate how an author's choice of point of view or perspective shapes style and meaning within and across literary texts.
description_plain: Your child understands how an author's choice of who tells the story changes how the story feels and what it means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify and explain a central idea and supporting details in an informational text.
description_plain: Your child can find the main idea in an informational text and explain the details that support it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify and explain an explicit theme in a literary text.
description_plain: Your child can identify the main message or lesson in a story and explain it clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate and critique the development of themes and central ideas within and across texts.
description_plain: Your child can think about and talk about how themes and main ideas develop in one or more texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify and explain the purpose of forms of figurative language to include similes, personification, and idioms.
description_plain: Your child can recognize and explain why authors use similes, personification, and idioms to make writing more interesting.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe the main story elements and sequence the important events.
description_plain: Your child can describe the main parts of a story and put the important events in the right order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate and critique key literary elements that enhance and deepen meaning within and across texts.
description_plain: Your child can think about and explain important story parts that make the story more meaningful.

subject: Language Arts
description: using context and visuals from the text to support monitoring and self-correcting.
description_plain: Your child uses clues from the words and pictures to check their reading and fix mistakes.

subject: Language Arts
description: generalizing phonic skills to unknown words; and
description_plain: Your child uses what they know about sounds and letters to read new words.

subject: Language Arts
description: recognizing chunks;
description_plain: recognizing chunks;

subject: Language Arts
description: decoding the words by analogy;
description_plain: Your child reads new words by comparing them to words they already know.

subject: Language Arts
description: using letter-sound knowledge to segment and blend sounds together;
description_plain: Your child uses letter sounds to break words into parts and put the sounds together to read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read texts by:
description_plain: Read texts by:

subject: Language Arts
description: Read texts orally and silently with accuracy, appropriate rate, expression, and intonation.
description_plain: Your child reads out loud and silently with accuracy, good speed, and expression.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read high-frequency words commonly found in grade-appropriate text with accuracy and automaticity.
description_plain: Your child reads common words for their grade quickly and correctly without needing to stop.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and reread grade-appropriate texts with accuracy and expression at an appropriate rate to support comprehension.
description_plain: Your child reads and rereads texts at their grade level accurately and with expression to understand better.

subject: Language Arts
description: contractions with am, is, has, not, have, would, and will.
description_plain: Your child can read and write contractions like I'm, isn't, hasn't, don't, haven't, wouldn't, and will.

subject: Language Arts
description: words with silent letter combinations. (e.g., kn, wr, gn, mb, gh); and
description_plain: Your child can recognize and read words that have silent letter pairs like kn, wr, gn, mb, and gh.

subject: Language Arts
description: words with vowel y in medial position, producing the short /ĭ/ sound for these words (e.g., fly-my; baby-happy; myth-gym);
description_plain: Your child understands that the letter y in the middle of words can make a short 'i' sound, like in fly, baby, and myth.

subject: Language Arts
description: words with vowel y in the final position of one- and two-syllable words, distinguishing the difference between the long /ī/ sound in one-syllable words and the long /ē/ sound in two-syllable words;
description_plain: Your child knows that y at the end of one-syllable words sounds like a long 'i' (like fly) and at the end of two-syllable words sounds like a long 'e' (like happy).

subject: Language Arts
description: words with the hard and soft sounds of c and g, in context and in isolation. (c=/k/ before a, o, u, or any consonant and c= /s/ before i, e, or y g=/g/ before a, o, u, or any consonant and g=/j/ before i, e, or y);
description_plain: Your child can tell when c and g make hard sounds (like cat and go) or soft sounds (like city and giant) depending on the letters that follow.

subject: Language Arts
description: words with a after w read /ä/ and a before l read /â/ (e.g., wash, water, wasp; tall, all, talk, small, fall);
description_plain: Your child can read words where 'a' sounds like 'aw' after w (like wash and wasp) and before l (like tall and fall).

subject: Language Arts
description: common inflectional endings that may change the base word (e.g., -ed, -ing, -s, -es);
description_plain: Your child can read and write words with endings like -ed, -ing, -s, and -es that change the meaning or form of the word.

subject: Language Arts
description: vowel-r combinations (ar, air, are, ear, eer, er, ere, eir, ir, or, oar, ore, our, ur);
description_plain: Your child can read words with vowel and r combinations like ar, air, er, or, and ur.

subject: Language Arts
description: variable vowel teams and vowel diphthongs (oi, oy; ou, ow; au, aw; oo, ew, ue; ee, ea; igh, ie; ai, ay);
description_plain: Your child can read words with vowel teams and vowel sounds that blend together, like oi, oy, ou, ow, au, aw, oo, ew, ue, ee, ea, igh, ie, ai, and ay.

subject: Language Arts
description: trigraphs (e.g., -tch, -dge);
description_plain: trigraphs (e.g., -tch, -dge);

subject: Language Arts
description: blends containing digraphs (e.g., thr-, -nch);
description_plain: Your child can read words that have blends with digraphs, like thr- in three and -nch in lunch.

subject: Language Arts
description: three-consonant blends (e.g., str-, scr-);
description_plain: Your child can read words with blends of three consonants together, like str- in street and scr- in scream.

subject: Language Arts
description: combination (e.g., qu);
description_plain: combination (e.g., qu);

subject: Language Arts
description: consonant digraphs (sh, th, ch, wh, ph, ck);
description_plain: Your child can read words with consonant pairs that make one sound, like sh, th, ch, wh, ph, and ck.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode and encode multisyllabic words using:
description_plain: Your child can break down and build longer words with more than one syllable by reading and spelling them.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read multisyllabic words by breaking the word into syllables.
description_plain: Your child can read longer words by breaking them into smaller parts called syllables.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delete, add, and substitute the initial, middle, and end letter combinations in words to build or make new multisyllabic words.
description_plain: Your child can change words by adding, removing, or swapping letters at the beginning, middle, or end to make new longer words.

subject: Language Arts
description: There is not an indicator for second grade.
description_plain: There is no learning goal for this standard in second grade.

subject: Language Arts
description: apply knowledge of multisyllabic word construction and syllable division principles to decode grade-appropriate multisyllabic words. Examples: rab-bit (CVC/CVC); o-pen (V/CVC); cab-in (CVC/VC); li-on (CV/VC).
description_plain: Your child can use rules about syllables to read longer words like rabbit, open, cabin, and lion by splitting them into parts.

subject: Language Arts
description: decode multisyllabic words with common syllable patterns (closed, open, vowel-consonant-e, vowel-r, vowel teams, consonant-le, and schwa syllables); and
description_plain: Your child can read longer words by recognizing common syllable types like closed, open, vowel-consonant-e, vowel-r, vowel teams, consonant-le, and schwa.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the vowel in a printed syllable or word to:
description_plain: Your child can find and say the vowel sound in a syllable or word when reading.

description_plain: Your child understands how books and print work, like reading left to right and top to bottom.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delete, add, and substitute initial, medial, and final phonemes of a spoken word up to six phonemes and say the resulting word.
description_plain: Your child can change sounds at the start, middle, or end of a word and say the new word.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate early phonological awareness to basic phonemic awareness in spoken words.
description_plain: Your child can hear and work with sounds in spoken words, like rhymes and syllables.

subject: Language Arts
description: Create quality work by adhering to an accepted format.
description_plain: Your child can create good work by following the proper format or style.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite evidence to explain and justify reasoning.
description_plain: Your child can find and use evidence to explain their ideas clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Collaborate with others and use active listening skills.
description_plain: Your child can work well with others and listen carefully during discussions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Make inferences to support comprehension.
description_plain: Your child can make guesses based on clues to better understand what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire, refine, and share knowledge through a variety of multimedia literacies to include written, oral, visual, digital, and interactive texts.
description_plain: Your child can learn and share information using different types of media like writing, speaking, pictures, and digital tools.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and write for a variety of purposes, including academic and personal, for extended periods of time.
description_plain: Your child can read and write for different reasons, like schoolwork or personal interests, and keep doing it for a long time.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify a speaker's claim and at least one supporting reason.
description_plain: Your child can tell what someone is trying to prove and give at least one reason they use.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate and critique ideas and concepts interactively through listening and speaking.
description_plain: Your child can listen and talk with others to share and think about ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: consider and reflect upon the ideas expressed during conversations.
description_plain: Your child thinks about what others say during conversations.

subject: Language Arts
description: enter a conversation appropriately, listen actively to others, and ask and respond to questions to clarify thinking and express new ideas; and
description_plain: Your child can join conversations politely, listen carefully, and ask or answer questions to understand and share new ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Participate in structured conversations and collaborations about grade-appropriate topics and texts:
description_plain: Your child will take part in guided talks and group work about topics and stories suitable for third grade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Through collaboration, react and respond to information while building upon the ideas of others and respecting diverse perspectives.
description_plain: Your child understands how to work with others by responding to ideas, adding their own thoughts, and respecting different opinions.

subject: Language Arts
description: identify and use appropriate digital tools to enhance verbal communication with support from adults.
description_plain: Your child can use helpful digital tools, with some adult help, to make their speaking clearer.

subject: Language Arts
description: demonstrate appropriate speaking techniques (e.g., nonverbal cues, appropriate volume and eye contact, and clear pronunciation) for a variety of purposes; and
description_plain: Your child can speak clearly using good volume, eye contact, and body language for different reasons.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present information orally to report on a topic or text, tell a story, or recount an experience with appropriate facts and relevant details. When presenting:
description_plain: Your child will share information out loud about a topic, story, or experience using facts and details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Organize and communicate ideas through a range of formats to engage a variety of audiences.
description_plain: Your child can organize and share ideas in different ways to interest different listeners.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write in cursive all uppercase and lowercase letters.
description_plain: Your child can write all uppercase and lowercase letters in cursive.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write independently and legibly for a variety of tasks and purposes.
description_plain: Your child can write neatly and on their own for different tasks and reasons.

subject: Language Arts
description: Improve writing by planning, revising, editing, and building on personal ideas and the ideas of others to strengthen writing.
description_plain: Your child can make their writing better by planning, fixing mistakes, and adding ideas from themselves and others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Revise writing to improve clarity and enhance style appropriate to audience, purpose, and task.
description_plain: Your child can improve their writing to make it clearer and more interesting for the reader and purpose.

subject: Language Arts
description: consult print and multimedia sources to check and correct spelling.
description_plain: Your child can use books and digital sources to check and fix spelling mistakes.

subject: Language Arts
description: identify and revise sentence fragments and run-on sentences; and
description_plain: Your child can find and fix incomplete sentences and sentences that run together without proper punctuation.

subject: Language Arts
description: distinguish between and use coordinating and subordinating conjunctions and independent and dependent clauses;
description_plain: Your child understands and uses words that join sentences and parts of sentences correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: explain the function of adjectives and adverbs in simple, compound, and complex sentences;
description_plain: Your child can explain how describing words (adjectives) and words that tell how actions happen (adverbs) work in different sentence types.

subject: Language Arts
description: use frequently occurring nouns, verbs (regular and irregular), and simple verb tenses;
description_plain: Your child can use common nouns and verbs, including regular and irregular verbs, in simple verb tenses.

subject: Language Arts
description: identify and use prepositional phrases;
description_plain: Your child can find and use phrases that show location, time, or direction (prepositional phrases).

subject: Language Arts
description: distinguish between and use comparative and superlative adverbs;
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between and use words that compare actions or qualities (comparative and superlative adverbs).

subject: Language Arts
description: distinguish between and use interrogative and demonstrative pronouns;
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between and use question words and words that point to things (interrogative and demonstrative pronouns).

subject: Language Arts
description: use apostrophes to create contractions;
description_plain: Your child can use apostrophes to make contractions like "don't" or "it's."

subject: Language Arts
description: use commas and quotation marks to indicate direct speech and quotations from a text; use commas to separate items in a series and phrases and clauses;
description_plain: Your child can use commas and quotation marks correctly when writing speech or quotes, and use commas to separate items in a list or parts of a sentence.

subject: Language Arts
description: use periods to punctuate abbreviations and within quotation marks;
description_plain: Your child can use periods correctly in abbreviations and inside quotation marks.

subject: Language Arts
description: capitalize nouns (organizations, monuments, and landmarks) and appropriate words in titles and dialogue;
description_plain: Your child understands when to capitalize names of organizations, monuments, landmarks, titles, and words in dialogue.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write and expand grammatically correct sentences (e.g., simple, compound, and complex) and paragraphs. When writing:
description_plain: Your child can write and make longer sentences and paragraphs that follow grammar rules, including simple, compound, and complex sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of standard English grammar and conventions when writing.
description_plain: Your child shows they know standard English grammar and punctuation when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: provide an ending.
description_plain: provide an ending.

subject: Language Arts
description: use descriptions of actions, thoughts, and feelings to develop characters; and
description_plain: Your child can describe what characters do, think, and feel to make them more real in stories.

subject: Language Arts
description: use temporal words and phrases to sequence a plot structure;
description_plain: Your child uses words that show time order to tell a story clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: establish a setting and introduce a narrator or characters;
description_plain: Your child can set the scene and introduce who is telling the story or who the characters are.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences. When writing:
description_plain: Your child can write stories about real or made-up events by including important story parts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences using effective techniques.
description_plain: Your child writes stories about real or imagined experiences using good storytelling techniques.

subject: Language Arts
description: provide a concluding statement or section.
description_plain: Your child can write a clear ending or conclusion to their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: use and explain information from a provided source; and
description_plain: Your child can use information from a source and explain it in their own words.

subject: Language Arts
description: use precise language and vocabulary to inform or explain about the topic;
description_plain: Your child uses clear and exact words to explain or inform about a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: group information and use grade-appropriate transitions to link ideas;
description_plain: Your child groups related information together and uses words that connect ideas smoothly.

subject: Language Arts
description: develop the topic with facts, definitions, and/or details related to the topic;
description_plain: Your child adds facts, definitions, or details to explain the topic well.

subject: Language Arts
description: introduce a topic;
description_plain: introduce a topic;

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory pieces to examine a topic and provide information. When writing:
description_plain: Your child writes informative pieces that explore a topic and give clear information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/expository texts to analyze and explain complex ideas and information.
description_plain: Your child writes informative texts that explain and analyze ideas and information clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: provide a concluding statement.
description_plain: Your child can write a clear ending statement in their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: organize information; and
description_plain: organize information; and

subject: Language Arts
description: use grade-appropriate transitions to link ideas;
description_plain: Your child uses words that help connect ideas in their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: include reasons supported by details from a provided source;
description_plain: Your child gives reasons and supports them with details from a source.

subject: Language Arts
description: introduce a topic; include an opinion statement;
description_plain: Your child can start writing about a topic and clearly say their opinion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write opinion pieces about a topic. When writing:
description_plain: Your child will write opinion pieces about a topic, sharing their thoughts clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims with clear reasons and relevant evidence.
description_plain: Your child understands how to write arguments that support their ideas with clear reasons and examples.

subject: Language Arts
description: Instruction of this indicator begins in fourth grade.
description_plain: This skill will be taught starting in fourth grade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Instruction of this indicator begins in fourth grade.
description_plain: This skill will be taught starting in fourth grade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Instruction of this indicator begins in fourth grade.
description_plain: This skill will be taught starting in fourth grade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Group findings from a provided print or non-print source.
description_plain: Your child can organize information found in books, videos, or other sources.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions about print and non-print sources to narrow or broaden thinking about a topic for inquiry.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions about books or other sources to learn more about a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use critical thinking skills to investigate, evaluate, and synthesize a variety of sources to obtain and refine knowledge.
description_plain: Your child uses thinking skills to explore and understand information from different sources.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify and explain how information gained from visuals (e.g., illustrations, photographs, graphs, maps) contributes to the meaning of a print or non-print text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how pictures, graphs, or maps help us understand a story or information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate and critique multimedia presentations of a text or subject, including their impact on an audience.
description_plain: Your child can watch or listen to presentations and talk about how they affect the audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify and use derivational morphemes, prefixes, suffixes, and phonic patterns to determine the meaning of words in grade-level content.
description_plain: Your child can use parts of words like prefixes and suffixes to figure out what new words mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Build and apply a range of academic vocabulary, as well as grade-level appropriate morphology to apply to reading, writing, and speaking.
description_plain: Your child learns and uses new vocabulary words in reading, writing, and speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: determine relationships between words (e.g., synonyms and antonyms) to better understand each of the words.
description_plain: Your child can find connections between words, like words that mean the same or opposite, to understand them better.

subject: Language Arts
description: identify real-life connections between words and their use (e.g., describe people who are helpful); and
description_plain: Your child can relate words to real-life examples, like describing helpful people.

subject: Language Arts
description: distinguish between literal and nonliteral meanings of words and phrases (e.g., take steps);
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between the literal and the figurative meanings of words and phrases.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine an author's use of words and phrases in grade-level literary, informational, and multimedia texts:
description_plain: Your child understands how authors use words and phrases in stories and information texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze word relationships and nuances in word meanings within literary and informational texts.
description_plain: Your child looks closely at how words relate to each other and what different meanings they can have.

subject: Language Arts
description: consult print and digital reference materials to find the pronunciation, part of speech, and to determine or clarify the precise meaning of words and phrases.
description_plain: Your child can use dictionaries or online tools to find how to say words, what kind of word they are, and what they mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: consult print and digital reference materials to build and integrate background knowledge; and
description_plain: Your child can use books or websites to learn more about topics and understand new information.

subject: Language Arts
description: use sentence-level context clues (e.g., definitions, examples) to determine the meaning of a word or phrase;
description_plain: Your child can use clues in a sentence, like definitions or examples, to figure out what a word or phrase means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of known and unknown words and phrases, choosing from an array of strategies:
description_plain: Your child will use different ways to understand the meaning of words and phrases they know and don't know.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases.
description_plain: Your child understands how to find the meaning of words that have more than one meaning or that are new to them.

subject: Language Arts
description: include a central idea and supporting details for an informational text.
description_plain: Your child can write an informational text that has a main idea and details that support it.

subject: Language Arts
description: include plot, theme, and key details for a literary text; and
description_plain: Your child can include the plot, theme, and important details when writing about a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Summarize a text to enhance comprehension:
description_plain: Your child can summarize a text to help understand it better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Summarize and paraphrase text to support comprehension and understanding.
description_plain: Your child can retell or put a text into their own words to understand it more clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify an author's claim, and explain how an author uses reasons to support that claim in an informational text.
description_plain: Your child can find the author's main point and explain how the author uses reasons to support it in an informational text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how basic text features contribute to meaning in an informational text; identify the text structures of description/list and/or cause and effect.
description_plain: Your child can explain how parts of an informational text, like headings or lists, help the meaning and can identify if the text shows description or cause and effect.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how sections of literary text (e.g., paragraphs, chapters, scenes, and stanzas) build on one another and contribute to the overall structure.
description_plain: Your child can explain how parts of a story, like paragraphs or chapters, work together to build the whole story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate and critique how an author uses words, phrases, and text structures to craft text.
description_plain: Your child can think about and explain how an author uses words and text structure to create meaning in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine and explain an author's purpose (e.g., what an author wants to answer, explain, or describe); identify an author's perspective on a topic.
description_plain: Your child can figure out and explain why an author wrote a text and what the author thinks about the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate and critique how an author's perspective and purpose shape style and meaning within and across informational texts.
description_plain: Your child can think about how an author's purpose and point of view affect the style and meaning of informational texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine and explain the differences between the perspectives of the characters and/or between the characters and the reader.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between what characters think and feel and how that is different from the reader's view.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate how an author's choice of point of view or perspective shapes style and meaning within and across literary texts.
description_plain: Your child can explain how an author's choice of point of view changes the style and meaning of stories.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine and explain a stated central idea and supporting details in an informational text.
description_plain: Your child can find and explain the main idea and supporting details in an informational text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify and explain an explicit theme in a literary text and how it is developed by key details.
description_plain: Your child can identify the main theme in a story and explain how important details help develop it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate and critique the development of themes and central ideas within and across texts.
description_plain: Your child can think about and explain how themes and main ideas develop in one or more texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify and explain the purpose of forms of figurative language to include metaphor, hyperbole, and idioms.
description_plain: Your child can recognize and explain why authors use figurative language like metaphors, exaggerations, and idioms.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how one or more characters develop throughout the plot.
description_plain: Your child can explain how one or more characters change or grow during a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate and critique key literary elements that enhance and deepen meaning within and across texts.
description_plain: Your child can talk about important parts of stories and explain how they make the story more meaningful.

subject: Language Arts
description: using context and visuals from the text to support monitoring and self-correcting.
description_plain: Your child uses clues from the words and pictures to check their reading and fix mistakes.

subject: Language Arts
description: generalizing phonic skills to unknown words; and
description_plain: Your child uses what they know about sounds to figure out new words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate early phonological awareness to basic phonemic awareness in spoken words.
description_plain: Your child can hear and play with sounds in spoken words, like recognizing rhymes and beginning sounds.

subject: Language Arts
description: Create quality work by adhering to an accepted format.
description_plain: Your child can create good work by following the proper format or style.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite evidence to explain and justify reasoning.
description_plain: Your child can find and use evidence to explain their ideas clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Collaborate with others and use active listening skills.
description_plain: Your child can work well with others and listen carefully during discussions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Make inferences to support comprehension.
description_plain: Your child can make guesses based on clues to better understand what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire, refine, and share knowledge through a variety of multimedia literacies to include written, oral, visual, digital, and interactive texts.
description_plain: Your child can learn and share information using different types of media like writing, speaking, pictures, and digital tools.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and write for a variety of purposes, including academic and personal, for extended periods of time.
description_plain: Your child can read and write for different reasons, like schoolwork or personal interests, and keep doing it for a long time.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine if a speaker's argument is effective by identifying claims and explaining how they support the argument.
description_plain: Your child can tell if a speaker’s argument makes sense by finding the main points and explaining how they support the idea.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate and critique ideas and concepts interactively through listening and speaking.
description_plain: Your child will listen and talk with others to share and think about different ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: consider and reflect upon the ideas expressed during conversations to demonstrate an understanding of diverse perspectives.
description_plain: Your child thinks about what others say during conversations to understand different points of view.

subject: Language Arts
description: engage in focused conversation about grade-appropriate topics and texts; build on the ideas of others, pose and respond to questions to clarify thinking, and express new ideas; and
description_plain: Your child joins in focused talks about topics or books, asks and answers questions, and shares new ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Participate in structured discussions and collaborations about grade-appropriate topics and texts:
description_plain: Your child takes part in organized talks and group work about topics and books suitable for fifth grade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Through collaboration, react and respond to information while building upon the ideas of others and respecting diverse perspectives.
description_plain: Your child works with others, listens to their ideas, shares their own, and respects different opinions.

subject: Language Arts
description: use digital tools to produce multimedia texts and/or visual aids with support from peers and adults.
description_plain: Your child uses computers or other digital tools to create projects like videos or pictures with help from others.

subject: Language Arts
description: identify the purpose of the information being presented; and
description_plain: Your child can tell why information is being shared, like to inform, persuade, or entertain.

subject: Language Arts
description: demonstrate appropriate speaking techniques (e.g., appropriate eye contact, nonverbal cues, appropriate volume, clear pronunciation, pacing) for a variety of purposes and audiences;
description_plain: Your child can speak clearly using good eye contact, body language, volume, and pacing for different audiences and reasons.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present information orally to report on a topic or text, present an opinion, tell a story, or recount an experience, sequencing ideas logically and using appropriate and relevant facts. When presenting:
description_plain: Your child can talk about a topic, share an opinion, tell a story, or describe an experience in order, using facts that make sense.

subject: Language Arts
description: Organize and communicate ideas through a range of formats to engage a variety of audiences.
description_plain: Your child can organize and share ideas in different ways to interest different listeners or readers.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate fluent and legible cursive writing skills.
description_plain: Your child can write in cursive smoothly and neatly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write independently and legibly for a variety of tasks and purposes.
description_plain: Your child can write clearly and neatly on their own for different tasks.

subject: Language Arts
description: Improve writing by planning, revising, editing, and building on personal ideas and the ideas of others to strengthen writing.
description_plain: Your child can make their writing better by planning, fixing mistakes, and adding ideas from themselves and others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Revise writing to improve clarity and enhance style appropriate to audience, purpose, and task.
description_plain: Your child can improve their writing to make it clearer and more interesting for the reader and purpose.

subject: Language Arts
description: consult print and multimedia sources to check and correct spelling.
description_plain: Your child can use books and online tools to check and fix spelling mistakes.

subject: Language Arts
description: identify and revise sentence fragments, run-on sentences, and inappropriate shifts in verb tense; and
description_plain: Your child can find and fix incomplete sentences, run-on sentences, and wrong verb tenses.

subject: Language Arts
description: use correct subject-verb and pronoun-antecedent agreement;
description_plain: Your child can use subjects and verbs that match and make sure pronouns agree with the nouns they replace.

subject: Language Arts
description: use prepositional phrases in different positions within a sentence;
description_plain: Your child can use phrases that show location or time in different parts of a sentence.

subject: Language Arts
description: distinguish between and use types of pronouns (indefinite) and pronouns as adjectives (possessive and demonstrative);
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between types of pronouns and use them correctly, including possessive and demonstrative pronouns.

subject: Language Arts
description: explain the function of and use frequently occurring interjections, verb tenses (progressive perfect), and correlative conjunctions;
description_plain: Your child can explain and use common interjections, verb tenses that show ongoing or completed actions, and pairs of conjunctions like 'either/or'.

subject: Language Arts
description: use a colon to introduce a list and after a salutation in a business letter or email;
description_plain: Your child can use a colon to start a list and after greetings in letters or emails.

subject: Language Arts
description: use a comma to separate an introductory element from the rest of a sentence, to separate clauses, to set off a question tag, and to indicate direct address;
description_plain: Your child can use commas correctly after introductory words, between clauses, with question tags, and when directly talking to someone.

subject: Language Arts
description: use underlining, quotation marks, or italics to indicate titles of works;
description_plain: Your child can show titles of books, movies, or other works by underlining, using quotation marks, or italics.

subject: Language Arts
description: apply knowledge of rules for capitalization;
description_plain: Your child can use capital letters correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write grammatically correct single and multi-paragraph compositions using a variety of sentence types and phrasing. When writing:
description_plain: Your child can write correct sentences and paragraphs using different sentence types and word choices.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of standard English grammar and conventions when writing.
description_plain: Your child can use proper grammar and writing rules when they write.

subject: Language Arts
description: provide an ending that follows logically from the narrated experiences or events.
description_plain: Your child can write an ending that makes sense based on the story or events they shared.

subject: Language Arts
description: use a variety of transitional words and phrases to sequence the events; and
description_plain: Your child can use different words and phrases to show the order of events in a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: use narrative techniques such as dialogue, precise words, descriptive language, and sensory details to develop events, setting, and characters;
description_plain: Your child can add dialogue, exact words, and detailed descriptions to make the story’s events, places, and characters come alive.

subject: Language Arts
description: establish a plot structure;
description_plain: establish a plot structure;

subject: Language Arts
description: introduce a narrator and characters;
description_plain: Your child can introduce who is telling the story and the characters involved.

subject: Language Arts
description: establish a situation and setting;
description_plain: Your child can start a story by explaining what is happening and where it takes place.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences. When writing:
description_plain: Your child will write stories about real or made-up experiences using important writing steps.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences using effective techniques.
description_plain: Your child will write stories about real or imagined events using good writing techniques.

subject: Language Arts
description: provide a concluding statement or section.
description_plain: Your child can write an ending that wraps up the information clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: use and explain information from a provided source(s); and
description_plain: Your child can use facts from given sources and explain them in their own words.

subject: Language Arts
description: use precise language and vocabulary to explain the topic;
description_plain: Your child can use clear and exact words to explain the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: group related information into paragraphs and/or sections using transitional words and phrases to link ideas and develop structure;
description_plain: Your child can organize related information into paragraphs and use linking words to connect ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: introduce a topic and develop the topic with facts, definitions, details, and/or quotes related to the topic;
description_plain: Your child can introduce a topic and add facts, definitions, details, or quotes to explain it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative texts to examine a topic and convey ideas and information clearly. When writing:
description_plain: Your child will write informative pieces that explain a topic clearly using facts and details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/expository texts to analyze and explain complex ideas and information.
description_plain: Your child will write informative texts that explain and analyze ideas and information.

subject: Language Arts
description: provide a concluding statement or section.
description_plain: Your child can write a conclusion that sums up their argument.

subject: Language Arts
description: use transitions to link and structure ideas; and
description_plain: Your child can use transition words to connect and organize their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: develop an organizational structure in which ideas are grouped logically;
description_plain: Your child can organize ideas in a clear and logical way.

subject: Language Arts
description: include reasons that are supported by facts from a credible source(s);
description_plain: Your child can give reasons supported by facts from trustworthy sources.

subject: Language Arts
description: introduce a topic and make a claim about the topic;
description_plain: Your child can introduce a topic and clearly state their opinion about it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to make a claim about a topic. When writing:
description_plain: Your child will write arguments that make a clear claim and support it with reasons and evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims with clear reasons and relevant evidence.
description_plain: Your child will write arguments that support their opinions with clear reasons and facts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite sources to avoid plagiarism.
description_plain: Your child can show where information comes from to avoid copying someone else's work.

subject: Language Arts
description: Logically group related findings.
description_plain: Your child can group related information together in a clear and logical way.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine which information is relevant to the topic.
description_plain: Your child can figure out which information is important for the topic they are studying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the credibility of the source(s) consulted and use the most credible source(s).
description_plain: Your child can decide if a source is trustworthy and choose the best ones to use.

subject: Language Arts
description: consulting a variety of print and multimedia sources.
description_plain: Your child can look at different types of books, videos, and other media to find information.

subject: Language Arts
description: generating a question(s) about a topic; and
description_plain: Your child can come up with questions about a topic they want to learn more about.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short research for inquiry by:
description_plain: Your child can do short research by asking questions and using different sources to find answers.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use critical thinking skills to investigate, evaluate, and synthesize a variety of sources to obtain and refine knowledge.
description_plain: Your child understands how to think carefully about different sources to learn and improve their knowledge.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast a print format of a text (e.g., literary, informational) to a non-print format (e.g., film, image, performance), explaining each media's portrayal of the subject.
description_plain: Your child can compare a book or article to a movie or picture about the same topic and explain how each shows the subject.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate and critique multimedia presentations of a text or subject, including their impact on an audience.
description_plain: Your child can watch or listen to presentations about a topic and talk about how they affect the audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify and use knowledge of affixes and Greek and Latin roots to determine new and/or clarify precise meanings of words and phrases in grade-appropriate content.
description_plain: Your child can use knowledge of word parts like prefixes and roots to understand new words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Build and apply a range of academic vocabulary, as well as grade-level appropriate morphology to apply to reading, writing, and speaking.
description_plain: Your child can learn and use new academic words when reading, writing, and speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: demonstrate knowledge of relationships between particular words (e.g., synonyms, antonyms, homographs) to better understand each of the words.
description_plain: Your child can recognize and explain how words are related, like words that mean the same or opposite.

subject: Language Arts
description: recognize and explain the meaning of figurative language, including similes and metaphors, in context; and
description_plain: Your child can understand and explain similes and metaphors when reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine an author's use of figurative and technical language in literary, informational, and multimedia texts:
description_plain: Your child can notice and explain how authors use special language like figurative or technical words in different texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze word relationships and nuances in word meanings within literary and informational texts.
description_plain: Your child can explore how words relate to each other and understand subtle differences in their meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: consult print and digital reference materials to find the pronunciation, part of speech, and to determine or clarify the precise meaning of words and phrases.
description_plain: Your child can use dictionaries and other resources to find how to say a word, what kind of word it is, and what it means.

subject: Language Arts
description: consult print and digital reference materials to build and integrate background knowledge; and
description_plain: Your child can use books and online tools to learn more about a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: use context clues (e.g., definitions, examples, restatements) to determine the meaning of words and phrases;
description_plain: Your child can use clues in sentences to figure out what words mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of known, unknown, and multiple-meaning words and phrases, choosing from an array of strategies:
description_plain: Your child can find or understand the meaning of words by using different strategies like context clues and reference materials.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of new or tricky words and phrases in what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: include a central idea and relevant supporting details for an informational text.
description_plain: Your child will write informational texts that include a main idea and important supporting details.

subject: Language Arts
description: include plot, theme, and relevant key details for a literary text; and
description_plain: Your child will include the plot, theme, and important details when writing about stories or literary texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Summarize a text to enhance comprehension:
description_plain: Your child can summarize a text to better understand what it means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Summarize and paraphrase text to support comprehension and understanding.
description_plain: Your child can summarize or put text into their own words to help understand it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine an author's argument and trace its development while identifying claims, supporting reasons, and evidence in an informational text.
description_plain: Your child can find the author's main argument in an informational text and explain the reasons and evidence used to support it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast how text features and/or structures contribute to the overall meaning in two or more informational texts.
description_plain: Your child can compare how different parts or features of two or more informational texts help explain their meaning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how a sequence of paragraphs, chapters, scenes, or stanzas fit together to provide the overall structure of a narrative, drama, or poem.
description_plain: Your child can explain how paragraphs, chapters, or scenes work together to create the structure of a story, play, or poem.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate and critique how an author uses words, phrases, and text structures to craft text.
description_plain: Your child can think about and explain how an author uses words and text structure to create meaning in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast a primary account and a secondary account of the same event or topic, while identifying how the different perspectives impact the content of the text.
description_plain: Your child can compare a firsthand account and a secondhand account of the same event and explain how their different views change the story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate and critique how an author's perspective and purpose shape style and meaning within and across informational texts.
description_plain: Your child can explain how an author's viewpoint and purpose affect the style and meaning of informational texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: explain how an author reveals one or more characters' perspectives in a literary text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how an author shows what a character thinks or feels in a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how the point of view from which a story is narrated influences how events are described; and
description_plain: Your child can explain how the narrator's point of view changes how the story's events are told.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate how an author's choice of point of view or perspective shapes style and meaning within and across literary texts.
description_plain: Your child can explain how an author's choice of point of view affects the style and meaning of stories.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain the development of a stated or implied central idea over the course of an informational text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how the main idea develops throughout an informational text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain the development of an explicit or implied theme over the course of a literary text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how a theme develops throughout a story or literary text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate and critique the development of themes and central ideas within and across texts.
description_plain: Your child can think about and explain how themes and main ideas develop in different texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how figurative language impacts meaning and contributes to the reader's experience.
description_plain: Your child can explain how figurative language (like similes or metaphors) adds meaning and makes reading more interesting.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how setting, characters, and conflict impact plot development.
description_plain: Your child can explain how the setting, characters, and conflicts affect what happens in a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate and critique key literary elements that enhance and deepen meaning within and across texts.
description_plain: Your child can think about and explain important parts of stories that make them more meaningful.
