subject: Language Arts
description: Present information orally to report on a topic or text, present an opinion, tell a story, or recount an experience, sequencing ideas logically and using relevant facts, descriptions, and details. When presenting:
description_plain: Your child can speak clearly to share information, give opinions, tell stories, or describe experiences in order, using facts and details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Organize and communicate ideas through a range of formats to engage a variety of audiences.
description_plain: Your child can organize ideas and share them in different ways to interest different listeners or readers.

subject: Language Arts
description: This standard is not directly addressed after fifth grade.
description_plain: This skill is not taught after fifth grade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write independently and legibly for a variety of tasks and purposes.
description_plain: Your child will write neatly and on their own for different tasks and reasons.

subject: Language Arts
description: Improve writing by planning, editing, and considering feedback from adults and peers and revising for clarity of content.
description_plain: Your child can make their writing better by planning, fixing mistakes, listening to feedback, and making changes to make ideas clearer.

subject: Language Arts
description: Revise writing to improve clarity and enhance style appropriate to audience, purpose, and task.
description_plain: Your child revises their writing to make it clearer and more interesting for the reader and purpose.

subject: Language Arts
description: consult print and multimedia sources to check and correct spelling.
description_plain: Your child uses books and online tools to check and fix spelling mistakes.

subject: Language Arts
description: identify and revise sentence fragments, run-on sentences, pronoun-antecedent agreement, and inappropriate shifts in verb tense; and
description_plain: Your child can find and fix sentence mistakes like fragments, run-ons, pronoun errors, and wrong verb tenses.

subject: Language Arts
description: distinguish between and use different types of phrases (prepositional and appositive);
description_plain: Your child knows different types of phrases and uses them correctly in writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: distinguish between and use simple, compound, complex, and compound-complex sentences to add variety;
description_plain: Your child uses different kinds of sentences to make writing more interesting.

subject: Language Arts
description: explain the function of definite and indefinite articles and apply to writing compound-complex sentences to add variety;
description_plain: Your child understands how to use 'the' and 'a/an' and writes complex sentences to add variety.

subject: Language Arts
description: determine and correct pronouns with unclear or ambiguous antecedents;
description_plain: Your child can find and fix unclear pronouns in their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: use a colon to introduce a quotation and a semicolon to link related independent clauses; use a hyphen in a compound adjective with numbers (e.g., two-hour movie, 30-minute class);
description_plain: Your child uses colons, semicolons, and hyphens correctly in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: use punctuation (commas, parentheses, dashes) to set off nonrestrictive clauses;
description_plain: Your child uses commas, parentheses, and dashes to set off extra information in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: apply knowledge of rules for capitalization;
description_plain: Your child knows and uses capitalization rules correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use the rules of and make intentional decisions about standard English grammar and conventions to write single and multi-paragraph compositions with varied sentence structure. When writing:
description_plain: Your child uses grammar rules and makes smart choices to write paragraphs with different sentence types.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of standard English grammar and conventions when writing.
description_plain: Your child shows they understand grammar and writing rules when they write.

subject: Language Arts
description: provide an ending that follows logically from the narrated experiences or events.
description_plain: Your child writes endings that make sense based on the story or events.

subject: Language Arts
description: use precise words and phrases, relevant descriptive details, and figurative language to convey experiences and events; and
description_plain: Your child uses clear words, details, and creative language to describe experiences and events.

subject: Language Arts
description: use narrative techniques such as dialogue and description to develop experiences, events, setting, and/or characters;
description_plain: Your child uses dialogue and descriptions to develop the story’s events, setting, and characters.

subject: Language Arts
description: organize a logical plot structure;
description_plain: Your child organizes their story with a clear and logical sequence of events.

subject: Language Arts
description: establish context and introduce a narrator and/or characters;
description_plain: Your child can set the scene and introduce who is telling the story and the characters involved.

subject: Language Arts
description: produce clear and coherent writing appropriate to the task and audience;
description_plain: Your child can write clearly and in a way that fits the purpose and who will read it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences, memories, or ideas, using effective techniques, relevant descriptive details, and well-structured event sequences. When writing:
description_plain: Your child can write stories about real or imagined events using good details and a clear order of events.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences using effective techniques.
description_plain: Your child understands how to write stories that show real or imagined experiences using good storytelling methods.

subject: Language Arts
description: provide a concluding statement or section.
description_plain: Your child can write a strong ending that wraps up their information.

subject: Language Arts
description: use precise language and vocabulary to inform or to explain the topic; and
description_plain: Your child uses exact words and vocabulary to explain or inform about a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: use appropriate transitions to clarify the relationships among ideas and concepts;
description_plain: Your child uses words that help connect ideas and make their writing easier to follow.

subject: Language Arts
description: develop the topic with relevant facts, definitions, details, quotes, or other information and examples;
description_plain: Your child adds facts, definitions, details, quotes, or examples to explain their topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: introduce a topic clearly and organize information logically;
description_plain: Your child clearly introduces a topic and organizes information in a logical way.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative texts to examine a topic and analyze information from one or more sources. When writing:
description_plain: Your child can write informative pieces that explore a topic and explain information from one or more sources.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/expository texts to analyze and explain complex ideas and information.
description_plain: Your child writes informative or explanatory texts that break down and explain complex ideas and information.

subject: Language Arts
description: provide a concluding statement or section that follows from the argument presented.
description_plain: Your child writes a conclusion that follows from the argument they made.

subject: Language Arts
description: use transitions to clarify the relationship(s) between claim and reasons;
description_plain: Your child uses connecting words to show how their reasons support their claim.

subject: Language Arts
description: use an organizational structure appropriate to the purpose and task;
description_plain: Your child organizes their writing in a way that fits the purpose and task.

subject: Language Arts
description: acknowledge an alternative perspective;
description_plain: Your child recognizes and mentions a different point of view.

subject: Language Arts
description: support a claim using logical reasoning supported by facts and/or data as evidence from a credible source(s);
description_plain: Your child supports their claim with logical reasons and facts or data from trustworthy sources.

subject: Language Arts
description: introduce a claim and organize the reasons and evidence clearly;
description_plain: Your child introduces a claim and clearly organizes the reasons and evidence that support it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support a claim with clear reasons and relevant evidence. When writing:
description_plain: Your child writes arguments that support a claim with clear reasons and relevant evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims with clear reasons and relevant evidence.
description_plain: Your child writes arguments to support their claims using clear reasons and evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite sources to avoid plagiarism.
description_plain: Your child knows how to give credit to sources to avoid copying someone else's work.

subject: Language Arts
description: Logically organize findings.
description_plain: Logically organize findings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine which source(s) and/or information is relevant to the topic.
description_plain: Your child can find which information or sources are important for the topic they are learning about.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the credibility of one or more sources consulted and use the most credible source(s).
description_plain: Your child can decide if a source is trustworthy and choose the best ones to use.

subject: Language Arts
description: using a variety of print and multimedia sources to refine the scope of inquiry as relevant to the topic.
description_plain: Your child can use different books, websites, and videos to narrow down their questions about a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: generating a question(s) about a topic; and
description_plain: Your child can come up with questions about a topic they want to learn more about.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short and more sustained research for inquiry by:
description_plain: Your child can do short or longer research projects by asking questions and finding information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use critical thinking skills to investigate, evaluate, and synthesize a variety of sources to obtain and refine knowledge.
description_plain: Your child understands how to think carefully about different sources to learn and improve their knowledge.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze a text or subject presented through multimedia formats (e.g., reading a print version of a speech and listening to an audio recording of a speech) and explain how each media's portrayal of the text or subject impacts the audience.
description_plain: Your child can compare how a story or speech is shown in different ways, like reading it and listening to it, and explain how each way affects people.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate and critique multimedia presentations of a text or subject, including their impact on an audience.
description_plain: Your child can judge and talk about how videos, audio, or other media show a story or topic and how it influences the audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of affixes and Greek and Latin roots to determine new and/or clarify precise meanings of words and phrases in grade-level content.
description_plain: Your child can use knowledge of word parts like prefixes and roots to figure out or better understand new words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Build and apply a range of academic vocabulary, as well as grade-level appropriate morphology to apply to reading, writing, and speaking.
description_plain: Your child builds and uses a variety of academic words and word parts when reading, writing, and speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: distinguish between the connotations of words with similar denotations (e.g., economical, thrifty).
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference in feelings or ideas between words that have similar basic meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: determine the relationship between words; and
description_plain: Your child can understand how words are related to each other.

subject: Language Arts
description: interpret figures of speech (e.g., metaphor) in context and analyze how they function within a text;
description_plain: Your child can explain what figures of speech like metaphors mean and how they work in a story or text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret an author's use of figurative, connotative, and technical language in literary, informational, and multimedia texts:
description_plain: Your child understands how authors use special language like metaphors, feelings behind words, and technical terms in different kinds of texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze word relationships and nuances in word meanings within literary and informational texts.
description_plain: Your child can look at how words relate and the small differences in their meanings in stories and information texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: consult reference materials to determine or clarify the precise meanings, pronunciations, or parts of speech of words and phrases.
description_plain: Your child can use dictionaries or other tools to find exact meanings, how to say words, or their parts of speech.

subject: Language Arts
description: use background or prior knowledge to determine or clarify the meanings of words and phrases; and
description_plain: Your child uses what they already know to understand or figure out the meanings of words.

subject: Language Arts
description: use context (e.g., the overall meaning of a sentence, paragraph, or text; a word's position or function in a sentence) to determine the meaning of words and phrases;
description_plain: Your child can use the surrounding words and sentences to figure out what a word means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of known, unknown, and multiple-meaning words and phrases, choosing from an array of strategies.
description_plain: Your child can figure out or clear up the meanings of words they know, don’t know, or that have more than one meaning by using different strategies.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases.
description_plain: Your child can understand or explain the meanings of words they don’t know or words with multiple meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Summarize and/or paraphrase content from grade-level text to enhance comprehension.
description_plain: Your child can retell or rewrite information from grade-level reading to better understand it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Summarize and paraphrase text to support comprehension and understanding.
description_plain: Your child will summarize and put text into their own words to help understand what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Trace the development of an author's argument while identifying the types of reasoning used and/or the rhetorical appeal of ethos, pathos, or logos in an informational text.
description_plain: Your child can follow an author's argument and recognize the reasons or emotional appeals they use in informational texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how individual text sections (e.g., a particular sentence, paragraph, chapter, or section) fit into the overall structure of an informational text.
description_plain: Your child understands how parts like sentences or paragraphs fit into the whole structure of an informational text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how a particular sentence, paragraph, chapter, scene, or stanza fits into the overall structure of a text and contributes to the development of theme, setting, or plot.
description_plain: Your child can explain how a sentence, paragraph, or scene helps build the story’s theme, setting, or plot.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate and critique how an author uses words, phrases, and text structures to craft text.
description_plain: Your child can think about and judge how an author uses words and text structure to create their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze a primary and secondary account of the same event or topic and how the different perspectives impact the content and style of a text.
description_plain: Your child can compare two accounts of the same event and explain how different viewpoints change the story and style.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate and critique how an author's perspective and purpose shape style and meaning within and across informational texts.
description_plain: Your child understands how an author’s viewpoint and purpose affect the style and meaning of informational texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine and explain the impact of multiple narrators or shifts in points of view and/or perspective.
description_plain: Your child can explain how having different narrators or changes in point of view affect a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate how an author's choice of point of view or perspective shapes style and meaning within and across literary texts.
description_plain: Your child understands how an author’s choice of viewpoint changes the style and meaning in stories.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how supporting details contribute to the development of a central idea(s) over the course of an informational text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how details support the main idea throughout an informational text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how key details contribute to the development of a theme(s) over the course of a literary text.
description_plain: Your child understands how important details help develop the theme in a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate and critique the development of themes and central ideas within and across texts.
description_plain: Your child can think about and judge how themes and main ideas develop in one or more texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how figurative language impacts mood, tone, and meaning.
description_plain: Your child can explain how figurative language (like similes or metaphors) affects the mood, tone, and meaning of a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how specific events and descriptive details develop the plot, reveal aspects of the characters, and/or create meaning.
description_plain: Your child can analyze how events and descriptions help develop the plot, show character traits, or create meaning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate and critique key literary elements that enhance and deepen meaning within and across texts.
description_plain: Your child can think about and judge important story elements that make texts more meaningful.

subject: Language Arts
description: Create quality work by adhering to an accepted format.
description_plain: Your child can create good work by following the proper format or style.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite evidence to explain and justify reasoning.
description_plain: Your child can find and use evidence to explain their ideas clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Collaborate with others and use active listening skills.
description_plain: Your child can work well with others and listen carefully during discussions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Make inferences to support comprehension.
description_plain: Your child can make guesses based on clues to better understand what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire, refine, and share knowledge through a variety of multimedia literacies to include written, oral, visual, digital, and interactive texts.
description_plain: Your child can learn and share information using different types of media like writing, speaking, pictures, and digital tools.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and write for a variety of purposes, including academic and personal, for extended periods of time.
description_plain: Your child can read and write for different reasons, like schoolwork or personal interests, and keep doing it for a long time.

subject: Language Arts
description: analyze a presentation to determine how a speaker articulates a clear message, chooses appropriate media, and uses appropriate voice for the audience.
description_plain: Your child can analyze a presentation to see how the speaker shares a clear message, picks the right media, and uses a fitting voice for the audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: determine the effectiveness of a speaker's argument and specific claims, evaluating the relevance of the evidence; and
description_plain: Your child can decide how well a speaker’s argument works by looking at the claims and the evidence they use.

subject: Language Arts
description: Using digital media, an audio, video, or live presentation, and/or a printed transcript of a presentation:
description_plain: Your child can use digital media, audio, video, live presentations, or transcripts to understand and respond to presentations.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate and critique ideas and concepts interactively through listening and speaking.
description_plain: Your child can listen and speak to think about and discuss ideas and concepts with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: consider new ideas and diverse perspectives of others when forming opinions about a topic, text, or issue.
description_plain: Your child can think about new ideas and different points of view when deciding what they think about a topic or story.

subject: Language Arts
description: ask questions to build on ideas; acknowledge new information expressed by others, and when necessary, modify personal ideas; and
description_plain: Your child will ask questions to learn more, listen to what others say, and change their own ideas if needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Participate in structured discussions and collaborations about grade-appropriate topics and texts:
description_plain: Your child participates in organized talks and group work about topics and stories suitable for their grade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Through collaboration, react and respond to information while building upon the ideas of others and respecting diverse perspectives.
description_plain: Your child works with others by sharing ideas, responding to information, and respecting different opinions.

subject: Language Arts
description: select and use appropriate digital tools for the task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child chooses the right digital tools to complete a task for the right audience and purpose.

subject: Language Arts
description: include graphics and multimedia elements to aid comprehension, enhance communication, and add interest; and
description_plain: Your child adds pictures and videos to help explain ideas and make their work more interesting.

subject: Language Arts
description: convey a clear perspective with clear reasoning and valid evidence;
description_plain: Your child shares their opinion clearly and supports it with good reasons and facts.

subject: Language Arts
description: demonstrate and adjust speaking techniques (e.g., appropriate eye contact, nonverbal cues, adequate volume, clear pronunciation, pacing) for a variety of purposes and audiences;
description_plain: Your child uses good speaking skills like eye contact, clear voice, and proper speed when talking to different people.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present claims and findings, emphasizing key ideas in a focused manner with relevant descriptions, facts, details, and examples. When presenting:
description_plain: Your child presents main ideas clearly using facts, details, and examples when sharing information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Organize and communicate ideas through a range of formats to engage a variety of audiences.
description_plain: Your child organizes and shares ideas in different ways to keep different audiences interested.

subject: Language Arts
description: This standard is not directly addressed after fifth grade.
description_plain: This skill is not taught after fifth grade.

description_plain: Your child writes neatly and on their own for different tasks and reasons.

description_plain: Your child improves their writing by planning, editing, listening to feedback, and making their ideas clearer.

subject: Language Arts
description: Revise writing to improve clarity and enhance style appropriate to audience, purpose, and task.
description_plain: Your child revises their writing to make it clearer and better suited for who will read it and why.

subject: Language Arts
description: use print and multimedia resources to edit for grammar, usage, mechanics, and format in order to finalize drafts for a variety of publication media.
description_plain: Your child uses books and online tools to check and fix grammar, spelling, and formatting before finishing their work.

subject: Language Arts
description: identify and revise sentence fragments, run-on sentences, pronoun-antecedent agreement, and inappropriate shifts in verb tense; and
description_plain: Your child finds and fixes sentence mistakes like incomplete sentences, run-ons, wrong pronoun matches, and verb tense errors.

subject: Language Arts
description: use a comma to separate coordinate adjectives;

subject: Language Arts
description: apply knowledge of rules for capitalization;

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases.
description_plain: Your child can figure out or clear up the meanings of words they don’t know or words that have more than one meaning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Summarize and/or paraphrase content from grade-level text to enhance comprehension.
description_plain: Your child can retell or rewrite information from grade-level texts to help understand it better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Summarize and paraphrase text to support comprehension and understanding.
description_plain: Your child can summarize or put text into their own words to help understand what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Trace the development of an author's argument while explaining the types of reasoning and/or the rhetorical appeal of ethos, pathos, or logos in an informational text.
description_plain: Your child can follow an author’s argument and explain the reasons or emotional and logical appeals the author uses in informational texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how the structure of individual text sections (e.g., a particular sentence, paragraph, chapter, or section) and/or text features convey an author's purpose in an informational text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how parts of a text, like sentences or paragraphs, and text features help show the author’s purpose in informational writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how the structure of a literary text (e.g., narrative, drama, poem) contributes to its meaning.
description_plain: Your child can explain how the way a story, play, or poem is organized helps create its meaning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate and critique how an author uses words, phrases, and text structures to craft text.
description_plain: Your child can think about and explain how an author uses words, phrases, and text structure to create their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine an author's perspective or purpose in a text and determine how an author uses rhetoric to advance that perspective or purpose.
description_plain: Your child can figure out an author’s point of view or reason for writing and explain how the author uses language to support it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate and critique how an author's perspective and purpose shape style and meaning within and across informational texts.
description_plain: Your child can think about and explain how an author’s point of view and purpose affect the style and meaning of informational texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: analyze how an author uses point(s) of view to develop and contrast the perspectives of different characters.
description_plain: Your child can explain how an author shows different characters’ viewpoints by using different points of view.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain the influence of a narrator(s), including an unreliable narrator(s), and/or narrative shifts in point of view; and
description_plain: Your child can explain how a narrator, including one who may not be reliable, or changes in who is telling the story affect the story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate how an author's choice of point of view or perspective shapes style and meaning within and across literary texts.
description_plain: Your child can think about how an author’s choice of who tells the story or the perspective shapes the style and meaning in stories.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare two or more central ideas within an informational text(s) and how each is developed over the course of a text.
description_plain: Your child can compare two or more main ideas in informational texts and explain how each idea is developed throughout the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare two or more themes within a literary text(s) and how each is developed over the course of a text(s).
description_plain: Your child can compare two or more themes in stories and explain how each theme is developed throughout the story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate and critique the development of themes and central ideas within and across texts.
description_plain: Your child can think about and explain how themes and main ideas develop within and across different texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how figurative language impacts mood, tone, and meaning.
description_plain: Your child can explain how figurative language like metaphors or similes changes the feeling, tone, and meaning of a story or poem.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how lines of dialogue, specific events, or descriptive details develop the plot, reveal aspects of the characters, and/or create meaning.
description_plain: Your child can explain how dialogue, events, or descriptions help develop the story, show what characters are like, and add meaning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate and critique key literary elements that enhance and deepen meaning within and across texts.
description_plain: Your child understands important parts of stories and texts that make the meaning deeper and more interesting.

subject: Language Arts
description: Create quality work by adhering to an accepted format.
description_plain: Your child can create good work by following the proper format or style.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite evidence to explain and justify reasoning.
description_plain: Your child can find and use evidence to explain their ideas clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Collaborate with others and use active listening skills.
description_plain: Your child can work well with others and listen carefully during discussions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Make inferences to support comprehension.
description_plain: Your child can make guesses based on clues to better understand what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire, refine, and share knowledge through a variety of multimedia literacies to include written, oral, visual, digital, and interactive texts.
description_plain: Your child can learn and share information using different types of media like writing, speaking, pictures, and digital tools.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and write for a variety of purposes, including academic and personal, for extended periods of time.
description_plain: Your child can read and write for different reasons, like schoolwork or personal interests, and keep doing it for a long time.

subject: Language Arts
description: analyze the purpose of the information being presented.
description_plain: Your child can figure out why information is being shared in a presentation or text.

subject: Language Arts
description: analyze a presentation to determine how a speaker articulates a clear message, addresses possible misconceptions or objections, chooses appropriate media, and uses a style appropriate for the audience; and
description_plain: Your child can explain how a speaker shares a clear message, handles questions or doubts, picks the right tools, and uses a style that fits the audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: determine the effectiveness of a speaker's argument and specific claims, evaluating the speaker's reasoning and relevance of the evidence;
description_plain: Your child can decide how strong a speaker’s argument is by looking at their reasons and evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Using digital media, an audio, video, or live presentation, and/or a printed transcript of a presentation:
description_plain: Your child can listen to and understand presentations using digital media, videos, live talks, or written transcripts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate and critique ideas and concepts interactively through listening and speaking.
description_plain: Your child can listen and speak to share and think about ideas with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: consider new ideas and diverse perspectives of others when forming opinions about a topic, text, or issue; qualify or justify views based on evidence.
description_plain: Your child thinks about new ideas and different opinions when forming their own views and supports their opinions with evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: ask relevant questions to build on ideas; acknowledge new information expressed by others, and when necessary, modify personal ideas.
description_plain: Your child asks questions to learn more, listens to others’ ideas, and changes their own ideas when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Initiate and participate in structured discussions and collaborations about grade-appropriate topics and texts:
description_plain: Your child starts and takes part in organized talks and group work about topics and texts suitable for their grade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Through collaboration, react and respond to information while building upon the ideas of others and respecting diverse perspectives.
description_plain: Your child works with others by responding to ideas, building on them, and respecting different opinions.

subject: Language Arts
description: select and use appropriate digital tools.
description_plain: Your child can choose and use the right digital tools for their work.

subject: Language Arts
description: include graphics and multimedia elements to aid comprehension, enhance communication, and add interest; and
description_plain: Your child adds pictures and multimedia to help explain ideas, make communication clearer, and keep the audience interested.

subject: Language Arts
description: convey a clear perspective with clear reasoning and valid evidence;
description_plain: Your child shares their point of view clearly using good reasons and solid evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: demonstrate and adjust speaking techniques (e.g., appropriate eye contact, nonverbal cues, adequate volume, clear pronunciation, pacing);
description_plain: Your child shows and adjusts speaking skills like eye contact, body language, volume, clear speech, and pacing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present claims and findings, emphasizing key ideas in a focused manner with relevant descriptions, facts, details, and examples for a variety of tasks, purposes, and audiences. When presenting:
description_plain: Your child presents ideas and findings clearly with important details and examples for different tasks and audiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Organize and communicate ideas through a range of formats to engage a variety of audiences.
description_plain: Your child organizes and shares ideas in different ways to connect with many kinds of audiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: This standard is not directly addressed after fifth grade.
description_plain: This skill is not taught after fifth grade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write independently and legibly for a variety of tasks and purposes.
description_plain: Your child writes neatly and independently for different tasks and reasons.

subject: Language Arts
description: Improve writing by planning, editing, and considering feedback from adults and peers and revising for clarity and cohesiveness.
description_plain: Your child can make their writing better by planning, editing, listening to feedback from adults and friends, and fixing it to make it clear and easy to understand.

subject: Language Arts
description: Revise writing to improve clarity and enhance style appropriate to audience, purpose, and task.
description_plain: Your child will change their writing to make it clearer and more interesting for the people who will read it and the reason they are writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: use print and multimedia resources to edit for grammar, usage, mechanics, and format in order to finalize drafts for a variety of publication media.
description_plain: Your child uses books, websites, and other resources to check and fix grammar, spelling, punctuation, and layout before finishing their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: identify and revise sentence fragments, run-on sentences, and inappropriate shifts in verb tense, number, voice, and mood; and
description_plain: Your child can find and fix incomplete sentences, run-on sentences, and mistakes in verb tense, number, voice, and mood.

subject: Language Arts
description: use appropriate parallel structure in words, phrases, and clauses;
description_plain: Your child uses matching patterns in words and phrases to make their sentences clear and balanced.

subject: Language Arts
description: distinguish between and use active and passive voice and types of grammatical mood (indicative, subjunctive, conditional, imperative);
description_plain: Your child knows when to use active or passive voice and different verb moods like statements, wishes, conditions, or commands.

subject: Language Arts
description: use different types of verbals in sentences (gerunds, participles, infinitives);
description_plain: Your child uses special verb forms like gerunds, participles, and infinitives correctly in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: use a comma or dash to indicate a pause or break and an ellipsis to indicate an omission;
description_plain: Your child uses commas or dashes to show pauses or breaks and ellipses to show missing words.

subject: Language Arts
description: apply knowledge of rules for capitalization;
description_plain: Your child knows and uses the rules for capital letters correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use the rules of and make intentional decisions about standard English grammar and conventions to write compositions with varied sentence structures. When writing:
description_plain: Your child uses grammar rules and makes smart choices about sentence structure to write with different kinds of sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of standard English grammar and conventions when writing.
description_plain: Your child shows they understand and use correct grammar and writing rules when they write.

subject: Language Arts
description: provide an ending that connects to the intended purpose of the writing.
description_plain: Your child writes endings that match the purpose of their story or writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: use precise words and phrases, details, and figurative language to develop the experiences, events, setting, and/or characters; and
description_plain: Your child uses exact words, details, and creative language to describe experiences, events, places, or characters.

subject: Language Arts
description: use narrative techniques such as dialogue, pacing, and description to develop experiences, events, setting, and/or characters;
description_plain: Your child uses storytelling tools like dialogue, pacing, and description to develop events, settings, and characters.

subject: Language Arts
description: organize a logical plot structure and use a variety of transitional words and phrases to convey sequence and signal time shifts;
description_plain: Your child organizes their story with a clear plot and uses transition words to show the order of events and time changes.

subject: Language Arts
description: engage the reader by establishing a situation, a clear point of view, and by introducing a narrator and/or characters;
description_plain: Your child grabs the reader’s attention by setting up the situation, point of view, and introducing the narrator or characters.

subject: Language Arts
description: produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to the task, purpose, and audience;
description_plain: Your child writes clearly and logically with a style that fits the task, purpose, and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences, memories, or ideas, using effective techniques, relevant descriptive details, and logically structured event sequences. When writing:
description_plain: Your child writes stories about real or imagined experiences using good storytelling techniques, clear details, and a logical order of events.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences using effective techniques.
description_plain: Your child writes stories to share real or imagined experiences using effective writing methods.

subject: Language Arts
description: provide a concluding statement or section that supports the information presented.
description_plain: Your child writes a conclusion that supports and wraps up the information they shared.

subject: Language Arts
description: establish a tone appropriate to the task and audience; and
description_plain: Your child can choose the right tone for their writing depending on who will read it and what the writing is about.

subject: Language Arts
description: use precise language and thoughtful elaboration to inform or to explain the topic;
description_plain: Your child will use clear and exact words with good details to explain or inform about a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: use varied transitions to clarify the relationships between ideas and concepts;
description_plain: Your child can use different connecting words to show how ideas and information relate to each other.

subject: Language Arts
description: develop the topic with relevant facts, definitions, details, and/or quotes;
description_plain: Your child will add facts, definitions, details, or quotes that relate to the topic to make their writing stronger.

subject: Language Arts
description: introduce a topic clearly and organize ideas, concepts, and information, using a structure such as definition, compare and contrast, and/or cause and effect;
description_plain: Your child can clearly introduce a topic and organize their ideas using ways like definitions, comparisons, or cause and effect.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative texts to examine a topic and analyze information from multiple sources. When writing:
description_plain: Your child will write informative pieces that explore a topic and look at information from different sources.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/expository texts to analyze and explain complex ideas and information.
description_plain: Your child can write clear explanations about complex ideas and information.

subject: Language Arts
description: provide a concluding statement or section that supports the argument presented.
description_plain: Your child will write a strong conclusion that supports their argument.

subject: Language Arts
description: establish and maintain a tone appropriate to the task and audience; and
description_plain: Your child can keep the right tone in their writing based on the task and who will read it.

subject: Language Arts
description: use transitions to create cohesion and clarify the relationships between claim(s), a counterclaim, reasons, and evidence;
description_plain: Your child will use connecting words to link claims, counterclaims, reasons, and evidence clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: acknowledge and refute a counterclaim with relevant evidence; create an organizational structure that establishes relationships between reasons and evidence;
description_plain: Your child can recognize and explain opposing views using evidence and organize their reasons and evidence clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: introduce a claim(s) and support a claim(s) with logical reasoning and relevant evidence (e.g., facts, data, statistics) using accurate and credible sources;
description_plain: Your child will introduce their main claim and support it with clear reasons and trustworthy facts or data.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support a claim(s) with clear reasons and relevant evidence. When writing:
description_plain: Your child will write arguments that support their claims with clear reasons and relevant evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims with clear reasons and relevant evidence.
description_plain: Your child can write arguments that clearly explain and support their opinions with good reasons and evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite sources to avoid plagiarism.
description_plain: Your child will show where they got their information to avoid copying someone else's work.

subject: Language Arts
description: Logically organize findings as relevant to the purpose.
description_plain: Your child can organize their research findings in a way that fits the purpose of their work.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze findings to determine relevance to the topic.
description_plain: Your child will look at their research results and decide which information is important for their topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the credibility and relevance of sources using only those that are credible and relevant to the topic and the purpose of inquiry.
description_plain: Your child can check if their sources are trustworthy and useful for their research topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: using a variety of print and multimedia sources to refine the scope of inquiry as relevant to the topic and purpose.
description_plain: Your child will use different books, websites, and videos to focus their research on the topic and purpose.

subject: Language Arts
description: generating and answering a research question(s) about a topic; and
description_plain: Your child can come up with questions about a topic and find answers through research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short and more sustained research by:
description_plain: Your child can do short and longer research projects to find information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use critical thinking skills to investigate, evaluate, and synthesize a variety of sources to obtain and refine knowledge.
description_plain: Your child uses thinking skills to explore, judge, and combine information from different sources to learn more.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate a text or subject presented through multimedia formats (e.g., reading a print version of a scene in a play and watching a performance of a scene in a play), and analyze how each media's portrayal of the text or subject impacts the audience.
description_plain: Your child compares a story or topic shown in different ways, like reading and watching, and explains how each way affects the audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate and critique multimedia presentations of a text or subject, including their impact on an audience.
description_plain: Your child looks at and talks about how different presentations of a story or topic affect people watching or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of affixes and Greek and Latin roots to determine new and/or clarify precise meanings of words and phrases in grade-level content.
description_plain: Your child uses knowledge of word parts from Greek and Latin to figure out or clarify the meanings of new words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Build and apply a range of academic vocabulary, as well as grade-level appropriate morphology to apply to reading, writing, and speaking.
description_plain: Your child learns and uses new academic words and word parts when reading, writing, and speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: distinguish between the connotations of words with similar denotations (e.g., willful, resolute).
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference in feelings or ideas between words that have similar dictionary meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: determine the relationship between particular words to better understand each of the words; and
description_plain: Your child understands how certain words relate to each other to better understand their meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: interpret figures of speech (e.g., oxymoron) in context and analyze how they function within a text;
description_plain: Your child explains the meaning of expressions like oxymorons and how they work in a story or text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret an author's use of figurative, connotative, and technical language in literary, informational, and multimedia texts:
description_plain: Your child understands how an author uses special language, like figures of speech and technical words, in different kinds of texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze word relationships and nuances in word meanings within literary and informational texts.
description_plain: Your child looks closely at how words relate and the subtle differences in their meanings in stories and information texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: consult reference materials to determine or clarify the precise meanings, pronunciations, parts of speech, etymology, or standard usage of words.
description_plain: Your child uses dictionaries or other tools to find exact meanings, pronunciations, parts of speech, word origins, or correct usage of words.

subject: Language Arts
description: use background or prior knowledge to determine or clarify the meanings of words; and
description_plain: Your child uses what they already know to figure out or clear up the meanings of words.

subject: Language Arts
description: use context (e.g., the overall meaning of a sentence, paragraph, or text; a word's position or function in a sentence) to determine the meaning of words and phrases;
description_plain: Your child uses clues from sentences or paragraphs to understand the meaning of words and phrases.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of known, unknown, and multiple-meaning words and phrases, choosing from an array of strategies:
description_plain: Your child figures out or clears up the meanings of words, including those with more than one meaning, using different strategies.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases.
description_plain: Your child works on understanding the meanings of new or tricky words and phrases.

description_plain: Your child can retell or rewrite information from grade-level texts to better understand it.

subject: Language Arts

description_plain: Your child can explain how an author arranges a story, like using flashbacks or hints, to create feelings like mystery or suspense.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate and critique how an author uses words, phrases, and text structures to craft text.
description_plain: Your child can look closely at how an author uses words and text structure to make the story interesting and clear.

subject: Language Arts

description_plain: Your child understands an author's point of view or purpose and can explain how the author uses language to support it.

subject: Language Arts

description_plain: Your child can explain how an author's viewpoint and goals affect the style and meaning of informational texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how points of view and/or perspectives create effects to include suspense and dramatic irony.
description_plain: Your child can analyze how different points of view in a story create effects like suspense or surprise.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate how an author's choice of point of view or perspective shapes style and meaning within and across literary texts.
description_plain: Your child understands how an author's choice of perspective shapes the style and meaning in stories.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how supporting details contribute to the development of two or more central ideas within and across informational texts.
description_plain: Your child can explain how details help develop two or more main ideas in informational texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how key details contribute to the development of two or more themes within and across literary texts.
description_plain: Your child can explain how important details help develop two or more themes in stories.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate and critique the development of themes and central ideas within and across texts.
description_plain: Your child can evaluate how themes and main ideas grow and connect within and between texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how figurative language impacts mood, tone, and meaning in a text(s); explain an author's use of symbolism.
description_plain: Your child understands how figurative language affects the mood and meaning of a text and can explain the author's use of symbols.

description_plain: Your child can explain how important parts of a story work together to create its overall meaning.

description_plain: Your child can find and explain what a speaker is trying to prove and the reasons or facts they use to support it.

subject: Language Arts

description_plain: Your child can listen and talk with others to think about and judge different ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: consider and reflect upon the ideas expressed during conversations.
description_plain: Your child thinks about and understands the ideas shared during conversations.

subject: Language Arts
description: enter a conversation appropriately, listen actively to others, and ask and respond to questions to clarify thinking and express new ideas; and
description_plain: Your child can join conversations at the right time, listen carefully, and ask or answer questions to understand and share new ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Participate in structured discussions and collaborations about grade-appropriate topics and texts:
description_plain: Your child takes part in organized talks and group work about topics and stories suitable for their grade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Through collaboration, react and respond to information while building upon the ideas of others and respecting diverse perspectives.
description_plain: Your child works with others by responding to information, building on others' ideas, and respecting different opinions.

subject: Language Arts
description: identify and use appropriate digital tools to enhance verbal communication with support from adults.
description_plain: With some help, your child can use digital tools to make their speaking clearer and more interesting.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate appropriate speaking techniques (e.g., nonverbal cues, appropriate volume, and clear pronunciation) for a variety of purposes and audiences; and
description_plain: Your child can speak clearly using good body language, volume, and pronunciation for different reasons and listeners.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present information orally to report on a topic or text, tell a story, or recount an experience. When presenting:
description_plain: Your child can speak to share information, tell stories, or talk about experiences in a way that fits the topic and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Organize and communicate ideas through a range of formats to engage a variety of audiences.
description_plain: Your child can organize and share their ideas in different ways to keep different audiences interested.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate fluent and legible cursive writing skills.
description_plain: Your child can write in cursive neatly and smoothly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write independently and legibly for a variety of tasks and purposes.
description_plain: Your child can write clearly and neatly on their own for different tasks and reasons.

subject: Language Arts
description: Improve writing by planning, revising, editing, and building on personal ideas and the ideas of others to strengthen writing.
description_plain: Your child can make their writing better by planning, fixing mistakes, and adding ideas from themselves and others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Revise writing to improve clarity and enhance style appropriate to audience, purpose, and task.
description_plain: Your child can improve their writing to make it clearer and more interesting for the people who will read it and the reason it was written.

subject: Language Arts
description: consult print and multimedia sources to check and correct spelling.
description_plain: Your child can use books and digital sources to check and fix spelling mistakes.

subject: Language Arts
description: identify and revise sentence fragments and run-on sentences; and
description_plain: Your child can find and fix incomplete sentences and sentences that run together without proper punctuation.

subject: Language Arts
description: distinguish between frequently confused words (e.g., to, too, two; there, their, they're);
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between words that sound alike but have different meanings, like to, too, and two.

subject: Language Arts
description: use prepositional phrases;
description_plain: use prepositional phrases;

subject: Language Arts
description: order adjectives within sentences according to conventional patterns;
description_plain: Your child can put adjectives in the right order when describing things in a sentence.

subject: Language Arts
description: identify and use simple appositive phrases;
description_plain: Your child can find and use simple appositive phrases that rename a noun.

subject: Language Arts
description: use frequently occurring nouns (compound); distinguish between and use frequently occurring pronouns (relative), adverbs (relative), verbs (helping and linking), and proper adjectives;
description_plain: Your child can use common compound nouns and different types of pronouns, adverbs, verbs, and proper adjectives correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: use a comma before a coordinating conjunction in a compound sentence and with dependent clauses;
description_plain: Your child can use commas before words like 'and' or 'but' in compound sentences and with dependent clauses.

subject: Language Arts
description: capitalize nouns (historic periods, nationalities, languages) and proper adjectives;
description_plain: Your child knows to capitalize names of historic times, countries, languages, and related adjectives.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write grammatically correct single and multi-paragraph compositions using a variety of sentence types and phrasing. When writing:
description_plain: Your child can write correct sentences and paragraphs using different sentence types and ways of saying things.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of standard English grammar and conventions when writing.
description_plain: Your child understands and uses correct English grammar and writing rules.

subject: Language Arts
description: provide an ending that follows from the narrated experiences or events.
description_plain: Your child can write an ending that fits the story or events they told.

subject: Language Arts
description: use a variety of transitional words and phrases to sequence events; and
description_plain: Your child uses different words and phrases to show the order of events in their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: use narrative techniques such as dialogue, descriptive language, and sensory details to develop events, setting, and characters;
description_plain: Your child uses dialogue, detailed descriptions, and senses to make stories and characters come alive.

subject: Language Arts
description: organize a plot structure;
description_plain: organize a plot structure;

subject: Language Arts
description: introduce a narrator and/or characters;
description_plain: Your child can introduce who is telling the story and the characters involved.

subject: Language Arts
description: establish a situation and setting;
description_plain: Your child can start a story by explaining where and when it happens.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives developing real or imagined experiences. When writing:
description_plain: Your child can write stories about real or made-up experiences using good writing techniques.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences using effective techniques.
description_plain: Your child writes stories that show real or imagined events using effective storytelling skills.

subject: Language Arts
description: provide a concluding statement or section.
description_plain: Your child can write a clear ending or conclusion to their information writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: use and explain information from a provided source(s); and
description_plain: Your child can use information from sources and explain it in their own writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: use precise language and vocabulary to inform or explain about the topic;
description_plain: Your child uses clear and exact words to explain or give information about a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: group related information into paragraphs and/or sections using transitional words and phrases to link ideas;
description_plain: Your child groups related facts into paragraphs and uses words that connect ideas smoothly.

subject: Language Arts
description: develop the topic with facts, definitions, details, and/or quotes related to the topic;
description_plain: Your child adds facts, definitions, details, or quotes to explain their topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: introduce a topic clearly;
description_plain: introduce a topic clearly;

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine a topic and provide information. When writing:
description_plain: Your child can write informative texts that explain a topic clearly and with details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/expository texts to analyze and explain complex ideas and information.
description_plain: Your child can write clear explanations to help others understand complicated ideas and information.

subject: Language Arts
description: provide a concluding statement or section.
description_plain: Your child can write a closing sentence or paragraph that wraps up their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: group ideas logically using transitional words and phrases; and
description_plain: Your child can organize ideas in a logical order using words like 'first,' 'next,' and 'finally.'

subject: Language Arts
description: provide reasons supported by facts from a credible source(s); and
description_plain: Your child can give reasons for their opinion and support them with facts from trustworthy sources.

subject: Language Arts
description: introduce a topic and state an opinion about the topic;
description_plain: Your child can introduce a topic and clearly share their opinion about it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to make a claim about a topic. When writing:
description_plain: Your child can write to share their opinion about a topic by introducing it, giving reasons with facts, organizing ideas, and ending with a conclusion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims with clear reasons and relevant evidence.
description_plain: Your child can write to support their opinions with clear reasons and facts that relate to the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite sources to avoid plagiarism.
description_plain: Your child can show where they found information to avoid copying someone else's work.

subject: Language Arts
description: Group related findings.
description_plain: Group related findings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine which information is relevant to the topic.
description_plain: Your child can figure out which information is important and related to the topic they are learning about.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the credibility of a provided source.
description_plain: Your child can decide if a source of information is trustworthy.

subject: Language Arts
description: examining a provided source.
description_plain: examining a provided source.

subject: Language Arts
description: generating a question about a topic; and
description_plain: Your child can come up with questions about a topic they want to learn more about.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short research for inquiry by:
description_plain: Your child can do short research by asking questions and finding information to learn about a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use critical thinking skills to investigate, evaluate, and synthesize a variety of sources to obtain and refine knowledge.
description_plain: Your child can think carefully to explore, check, and combine information from different sources to learn more.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine and explain how visuals (e.g., illustrations, photographs, maps) and/or multimedia elements (e.g., video, audio) contribute to the meaning of a print or non-print text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how pictures, maps, videos, or sounds help make the meaning of a story or information clearer.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate and critique multimedia presentations of a text or subject, including their impact on an audience.
description_plain: Your child can think about and talk about how videos or other media show a story or topic and how they affect the audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify and use common Greek and Latin roots, base words, and affixes to determine the meaning of words in grade-appropriate content.
description_plain: Your child can use common word parts from Greek and Latin to figure out the meaning of new words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Build and apply a range of academic vocabulary, as well as grade-level appropriate morphology to apply to reading, writing, and speaking.
description_plain: Your child can learn and use new academic words and word parts when reading, writing, and speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: determine relationships between words (e.g., synonyms and antonyms) to better understand each of the words.
description_plain: Your child can understand how words are related, like words that mean the same or the opposite.

subject: Language Arts
description: explain the meaning of commonly occurring similes, metaphors, and idioms;
description_plain: Your child can explain what common similes, metaphors, and idioms mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: distinguish between literal and nonliteral meanings of words and phrases (e.g., take steps);
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between the literal meaning of words and phrases and their figurative or non-literal meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine an author's use of words and phrases in grade-level literary, informational, and multimedia texts:
description_plain: Your child can understand how an author uses words and phrases in stories, informational texts, and multimedia.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze word relationships and nuances in word meanings within literary and informational texts.
description_plain: Your child will explore how words relate to each other and notice small differences in word meanings in stories and information texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: consult print and digital reference materials to find the pronunciation, part of speech, and to determine or clarify the precise meaning of words and phrases.
description_plain: Your child can use books and online tools to find how to say a word, what kind of word it is, and what it means.

subject: Language Arts
description: consult print and digital reference materials to build and integrate background knowledge; and
description_plain: Your child uses books and digital resources to learn more about topics and understand what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: use context clues (e.g., definitions, examples, restatements) to determine the meaning of a word or phrase;
description_plain: Your child uses clues from the sentence or nearby sentences to figure out what a word or phrase means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of known, unknown, and multiple-meaning words and phrases, choosing from an array of strategies:
description_plain: Your child can find or explain the meaning of words they know, don’t know, or words with more than one meaning by using different strategies.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases.
description_plain: Your child works on understanding words they don’t know or words that have more than one meaning.

subject: Language Arts
description: include a central idea and relevant supporting details for an informational text.
description_plain: Your child can write or tell the main idea and important details in an informational text.

subject: Language Arts
description: include plot, theme, and relevant key details for a literary text; and
description_plain: Your child can include the story’s plot, theme, and important details when talking about a literary text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Summarize a text to enhance comprehension:
description_plain: Your child can summarize a story or text to better understand it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Summarize and paraphrase text to support comprehension and understanding.
description_plain: Your child can retell or explain a text in their own words to help understand it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain an author's use of reasons and evidence to support a claim(s) in an informational text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how an author uses reasons and facts to support their ideas in an informational text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how basic text features contribute to meaning in an informational text; identify the text structures of problem and solution, chronological, and/or compare and contrast.
description_plain: Your child can explain how parts of a text, like headings or timelines, help understand the information and can identify if the text shows problems and solutions, events in order, or comparisons.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the structural elements of literary texts (e.g., narratives, dramas, and poems).
description_plain: Your child can compare how different stories, plays, and poems are organized.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate and critique how an author uses words, phrases, and text structures to craft text.
description_plain: Your child can think about and talk about how an author uses words, phrases, and text structure to create meaning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine and explain how an author's purpose (e.g., what an author wants to answer, explain, or describe) is conveyed through the author's perspective.
description_plain: Your child can explain what the author wants to do or say and how the author’s point of view shows this.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate and critique how an author's perspective and purpose shape style and meaning within and across informational texts.
description_plain: Your child can think about how an author’s point of view and purpose affect the style and meaning in informational texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: explain how different characters' perspectives impact a literary text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how different characters’ views affect a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the point of view from which different stories are narrated, including the difference between first person and third person; and
description_plain: Your child can compare stories told from different points of view, like first person and third person.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate how an author's choice of point of view or perspective shapes style and meaning within and across literary texts.
description_plain: Your child can think about how an author’s choice of point of view or perspective changes the style and meaning in stories.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify and explain a stated or implied central idea and how it is developed by supporting details in an informational text.
description_plain: Your child can find the main idea in an informational text and explain how details support it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify and explain an explicit or implied theme and how it is developed by key details in a literary text.
description_plain: Your child can find the theme in a story and explain how important details help develop it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate and critique the development of themes and central ideas within and across texts.
description_plain: Your child can think about and talk about how themes and main ideas grow in one or more texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine and explain how figurative language impacts meaning and contributes to the reader's experience.
description_plain: Your child understands how special language, like metaphors or similes, changes the meaning and makes reading more interesting.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how setting and conflict cause characters to change and how conflict(s) contributes to plot development.
description_plain: Your child can explain how where and when a story happens and the problems characters face cause them to change and move the story forward.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate and critique key literary elements that enhance and deepen meaning within and across texts.
description_plain: Your child can notice and talk about important parts of stories that make the meaning deeper and more interesting.

description_plain: Your child can think carefully to understand and solve math problems using both ideas and numbers.

subject: Math
description: Analyze & Justify

description: Demonstrate a deep and flexible conceptual understanding of mathematical ideas, operations, and relationships while making real-world connections.Use critical thinking skills to reason both abstractly and quantitatively.
description_plain: Your child understands math ideas well and can connect them to real-life situations.

subject: Math
description: Explain ideas using precise and contextually appropriate mathematical language, tools, and models.
description_plain: Your child can explain math ideas clearly using the right words, tools, and pictures.

subject: Math
description: Representation & Communication
description_plain: Your child learns how to show and talk about math ideas.

subject: Math

subject: Math
