subject: Math
description: Mathematical Process Standards
description_plain: Your child learns important math thinking and problem-solving skills.

subject: Math
description: Recognize that a whole number is a multiple of each of its factors 1–10.
description_plain: Your child understands that a whole number can be divided evenly by each of its factors from 1 to 10.

subject: Math
description: Identify, create, and extend numerical patterns to determine the next three terms in an addition or subtraction sequence.
description_plain: Your child can find, make, and continue number patterns by adding or subtracting to find the next three numbers.

subject: Math
description: Solve one- and two-step real-world situations using addition and subtraction up to 1,000.
description_plain: Your child can solve real-life problems with one or two steps using addition and subtraction with numbers up to 1,000.

subject: Math
description: Determine the unknown whole number in a multiplication or division real-world situation relating three whole numbers when the unknown is a missing factor, product, dividend, divisor, or quotient.
description_plain: Your child can find a missing number in multiplication or division problems that involve three whole numbers.

subject: Math
description: Use reasoning to represent and solve algebraic and numerical situations.
description_plain: Your child uses thinking skills to show and solve math problems with numbers and simple equations.

subject: Math
description: Multiply two whole numbers from 0 to 10 and divide using related facts flexibly and accurately.
description_plain: Your child can multiply and divide numbers from 0 to 10 quickly and correctly using related facts.

subject: Math
description: Multiply whole numbers (factors 0–10) and divide whole numbers (divisors 1–10) using a model and write a corresponding equation.
description_plain: Your child can multiply and divide numbers from 0 to 10 using pictures or models and write the matching math sentence.

subject: Math
description: Use a strategy to compute sums and differences up to 1,000.
description_plain: Your child can use a plan to add and subtract numbers up to 1,000.

subject: Math
description: Use multiple representations to reason and solve problems involving operational properties of whole numbers.
description_plain: Your child can use different ways, like pictures or numbers, to solve problems with whole numbers and their operations.

subject: Math
description: Patterns, Algebra, and Functional Reasoning
description_plain: Your child understands patterns, simple algebra ideas, and how things change in math.

subject: Math
description: Compare two fractions with the same numerator or same denominator based on the same size whole by reasoning about their size. Use the symbols for is equal to (=), is less than (<), or is greater than (>). Limit denominators to 2, 3, 4, 6, and 8, and fractions should be limited to fractions between 0 and 1.
description_plain: Your child can compare two fractions with the same top or bottom number, decide which is bigger or smaller, and use <, >, or = signs with fractions between 0 and 1.

subject: Math
description: Recognize two fractions are equivalent based on the same size whole. Limit denominators to 2, 3, 4, 6, and 8, and fractions should be limited to fractions between 0 and 1.
description_plain: Your child can tell when two fractions are equal if they come from the same whole.

subject: Math
description: Compose fractions between the whole numbers 0 and 5 using unit fractions. Record the composition as a mixed number or fraction greater than 1. Limit denominators to 2, 3, 4, 6, and 8.
description_plain: Your child can make fractions between 0 and 5 by adding parts called unit fractions and write them as mixed numbers or improper fractions.

subject: Math
description: Express whole numbers as fractions and identify fractions that are equivalent to whole numbers. Limit denominators to 1, 2, 3, 4, 6, and 8.
description_plain: Your child can write whole numbers as fractions and find fractions that are the same as whole numbers.

subject: Math
description: Represent fractions from 0 to 1 using concrete, set, area, and linear models, and write them in standard form and word form. Limit denominators to 2, 3, 4, 6, and 8.
description_plain: Your child can show fractions between 0 and 1 using objects, pictures, or lines, and write them as numbers and words.

subject: Math
description: Identify unit fractions as the quantity formed by one part when a whole is partitioned into 2, 3, 4, 6, or 8 equal-sized parts. Express each part as a unit fraction of the whole.
description_plain: Your child understands that a unit fraction is one equal part when a whole is split into 2, 3, 4, 6, or 8 parts.

subject: Math
description: Represent and compare fractions in multiple ways using part-whole relationships.
description_plain: Your child can show and compare fractions in different ways by looking at parts of a whole.

subject: Math
description: Round whole numbers from 0 to 1,000 to the nearest 10 or 100.
description_plain: Your child can round numbers up to 1,000 to the nearest 10 or 100.

subject: Math
description: Compare two whole numbers up to 999,999 based on the place value of the digits using the symbols for is equal to (=), is less than (<), or is greater than (>).
description_plain: Your child can compare two numbers up to 999,999 using place value and the symbols =, <, or >.

subject: Math
description: Compose and decompose 4-digit whole numbers in multiple ways using thousands, hundreds, tens, and ones.
description_plain: Your child can break apart and put together 4-digit numbers using thousands, hundreds, tens, and ones.

subject: Math
description: Read, write, and represent whole numbers through the thousands period (0 to 999,999) on a number line and in standard, base ten language, word, and equations in expanded form.
description_plain: Your child can read, write, and show numbers up to 999,999 on a number line and in different forms like words and expanded form.

subject: Math
description: Represent and compare numbers using relationships within the base ten number system.
description_plain: Your child can show and compare numbers by understanding how the base ten system works.

subject: Math
description: Numerical Reasoning
description_plain: Numerical Reasoning

subject: Math
description: Identify, describe, and draw points, lines, line segments, rays, intersecting lines, perpendicular lines, and parallel lines. Identify these in two-dimensional figures.
description_plain: Your child can find, describe, and draw points, lines, line segments, rays, and different kinds of lines like intersecting, perpendicular, and parallel in flat shapes.

subject: Math
description: Describe and draw right, acute, obtuse, and straight angles. Identify these angle types in two-dimensional figures including triangles and quadrilaterals.
description_plain: Your child can describe and draw right, acute, obtuse, and straight angles and find these angles in shapes like triangles and squares.

subject: Math
description: Extend geometric reasoning to attributes of polygons and/or polyhedrons.
description_plain: Your child can use what they know about shapes to understand more about polygons and 3D shapes.

subject: Math
description: Determine which unit of liquid volume is most appropriate to measure in real-world situations. Limit to fluid ounces, cups, pints, quarts, gallons, milliliters, and liters.
description_plain: Your child can choose the best unit to measure liquid, like cups or liters, for real-life situations.

subject: Math
description: Estimate and measure length/distance to the nearest half inch and nearest whole centimeter.
description_plain: Your child can estimate and measure length or distance to the nearest half inch or whole centimeter.

subject: Math
description: Solve problems involving addition and subtraction of time intervals to determine elapsed time to the nearest half hour.
description_plain: Your child can solve problems by adding or subtracting time to find how much time has passed, rounding to the nearest half hour.

subject: Math
description: Use analog and digital clocks to tell and record time to 1-minute intervals, identifying AM and PM.
description_plain: Your child can tell and write the time to the exact minute using both analog and digital clocks, and understand the difference between AM and PM.

subject: Math
description: Determine the value of any collection of coins, not to exceed $5. Write the amount in the form of dollars and cents using the decimal notation. Limit to penny, nickel, dime, and quarter.
description_plain: Your child can count the value of coins up to $5 using pennies, nickels, dimes, and quarters, and write the amount using dollars and cents.

subject: Math
description: Estimate and measure using units of length, liquid volume, currency, and intervals of time.
description_plain: Your child can guess and measure length, liquid amounts, money, and time intervals.

subject: Math
description: Determine if a real-world situation is an example of the need for finding the area or the perimeter of a figure.
description_plain: Your child can decide if a real-life problem needs finding the area or the perimeter of a shape.

subject: Math
description: Determine the perimeter of regular and irregular triangles and quadrilaterals with known side lengths.
description_plain: Your child can find the perimeter by adding the side lengths of different triangles and four-sided shapes.

subject: Math
description: Determine the area of squares and rectangles presented in relevant problems by covering the space with square units and counting the total number of units needed.
description_plain: Your child can find the area of squares and rectangles by counting how many square units cover the shape.

subject: Math
description: Solve area and perimeter problems in real-world and mathematical situations.
description_plain: Your child can solve real-life and math problems that involve finding area and perimeter.

subject: Math
description: Measurement, Geometry, and Spatial Reasoning
description_plain: Your child understands measurement, shapes, and how to think about space.

subject: Math
description: Identify the possible outcomes of a simple event.
description_plain: Your child can list all the possible results of a simple event.

subject: Math
description: Represent the probability of simple events and determine possible outcomes.
description_plain: Your child can show the chance of simple events happening and find all possible results.

subject: Math
description: Solve one-step, real-world situations using whole number data represented in tables, scaled picture graphs, scaled bar graphs, or dot plots. Limit scales to multiples of 1, 2, 5, and 10.
description_plain: Your child can solve one-step real-life problems using numbers shown in tables, picture graphs, bar graphs, or dot plots with simple scales.

subject: Math
description: Collect and organize categorical and numerical data based on observations, surveys, experiments, and investigations with whole number values using tables, scaled picture graphs, scaled bar graphs, or dot plots. Use titles and labels. Limit scales to multiples of 1, 2, 5, and 10.
description_plain: Your child can gather and organize information from observations or surveys using tables, picture graphs, bar graphs, or dot plots with titles and labels.

subject: Math
description: Collect and analyze data and communicate through multiple representations.
description_plain: Your child can collect, study, and share information using different types of charts and graphs.

subject: Math
description: Data, Probability, and Statistical Reasoning
description_plain: Your child understands how to work with data, chance, and statistics.

subject: Math
description: Identify and apply regularity in repeated reasoning to make generalizations.
description_plain: Your child can notice patterns in repeated math steps and use them to make general rules.

subject: Math
description: Structures & Patterns
description_plain: Structures & Patterns

subject: Math
description: Use critical thinking skills to reason both abstractly and quantitatively.Identify and apply regularity in repeated reasoning to make generalizations.
description_plain: Your child can think carefully about math problems, notice patterns, and use these to make general rules.

subject: Math
description: Analyze & Justify
description_plain: Analyze & Justify

subject: Math
description: Demonstrate a deep and flexible conceptual understanding of mathematical ideas, operations, and relationships while making real-world connections.
description_plain: Your child understands math ideas well and can connect them to real-life situations.

subject: Math
description: Connections
description_plain: Connections

subject: Math
description: Explain ideas using precise and contextually appropriate mathematical language, tools, and models.
description_plain: Your child can explain math ideas clearly using the right words, tools, and pictures.

subject: Math
description: Representation & Communication
description_plain: Your child learns how to show and talk about math ideas.

subject: Math
description: Make sense of problems and persevere in solving them strategically.
description_plain: Your child can understand math problems and keep trying different ways to solve them.

subject: Math
description: Problem Solving
description_plain: Problem Solving

subject: Math
description: Mathematical Process Standards
description_plain: Your child learns important math thinking and problem-solving skills.

subject: Math
description: Solve two-step, real-world situations using the four operations involving whole number answers. Represent the problem using an equation with a variable as the unknown in any position.
description_plain: Your child can solve two-step real-life math problems using addition, subtraction, multiplication, or division and write an equation with a letter for the unknown.

subject: Math
description: Solve real-world situations involving multiplicative comparison situations and write equations to represent the problem using a variable for the unknown.
description_plain: Your child can solve real-life problems comparing amounts by multiplication and write equations using a letter for the unknown.

subject: Math
description: Describe and extend a numerical pattern that follows a rule using function tables and real-world situations.
description_plain: Your child can explain and continue number patterns using tables and real-life examples.

subject: Math
description: Find all factor pairs for a whole number in the range 1–50. Determine whether the whole number is prime or composite.
description_plain: Your child can find all pairs of numbers that multiply to a number between 1 and 50 and decide if that number is prime or composite.

subject: Math
description: Use reasoning to represent and solve algebraic and numerical situations.
description_plain: Your child can use thinking skills to write and solve math problems with numbers and simple equations.

subject: Math
description: Interpret a fraction as an equal sharing division situation, where a quantity (the numerator) is divided into equal parts (the denominator) to include real-world situations.
description_plain: Your child can understand a fraction as dividing something into equal parts and use this idea to solve real-life problems.

subject: Math
description: Represent and compute the product of a whole number times a unit fraction. Limit denominators to 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 8, 10, 12, 25, and 100.
description_plain: Your child can multiply a whole number by a simple fraction like 1/2 or 3/4 and find the answer.

subject: Math
description: Use fraction and decimal equivalencies to add and subtract tenths and hundredths, to include mixed numbers and fractions greater than 1.
description_plain: Your child can add and subtract tenths and hundredths using fractions and decimals, including numbers bigger than one.

subject: Math
description: Use a strategy to accurately compute sums and differences of fractions with like denominators and justify the reasonableness of the answer. Limit denominators to 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 8, 10, 12, 25, and 100.
description_plain: Your child can add and subtract fractions with the same bottom number and explain why their answer makes sense.

subject: Math
description: Use multiple representations to reason and solve problems involving operational properties of fractions.
description_plain: Your child can use different ways to understand and solve problems about how fractions work when you add, subtract, multiply, or divide.

subject: Math
description: Use a strategy to divide up to a four-digit dividend by a one-digit divisor, with and without remainders. Justify the calculation.
description_plain: Your child can divide numbers up to four digits by a one-digit number, with or without leftovers, and explain how they did it.

subject: Math
description: Decompose numbers by the value of each digit to multiply whole numbers up to four digits by a one-digit number and two 2- digit whole numbers.
description_plain: Your child can break apart numbers by place value to multiply big numbers by one-digit or two-digit numbers.

subject: Math
description: Compute the product of a one-digit whole number times a multiple of 10 (from 10 to 90) and 100 (from 100 to 900) based on place value and properties of operations.
description_plain: Your child can multiply a one-digit number by 10s or 100s (like 30 or 400) using place value ideas.

subject: Math
description: Use a strategy to accurately compute sums and differences of whole numbers up to 100,000 and justify the sum or difference.
description_plain: Your child can add and subtract whole numbers up to 100,000 and explain why their answer is correct.

subject: Math
description: Use multiple representations to reason and solve problems involving operational properties of whole numbers and decimals.
description_plain: Your child can use different methods to understand and solve problems with whole numbers and decimals.

subject: Math
description: Patterns, Algebra, and Functional Reasoning
description_plain: Your child learns about patterns, algebra, and how numbers work together.

subject: Math
description: Compare fractions and mixed numbers with like and unlike denominators applying benchmark fractions such as 0, 1 2 , and 1 using the symbols for is equal to (=), is less than (<), or is greater than (>). Limit fractions to denominators of 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 8, 10, 12, 20, 25, 50, and 100.
description_plain: Your child can compare fractions and mixed numbers using symbols like =, <, and >, even when the bottom numbers are different.

subject: Math
description: Explain and demonstrate how a mixed number is equivalent to a fraction greater than 1 and how a fraction greater than 1 is equivalent to a mixed number. Limit fractions to denominators of 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 8, 10, 12, 20, 25, 50, and 100.
description_plain: Your child can explain and show how a mixed number is the same as a fraction bigger than one, and vice versa.

subject: Math
description: Represent the composition and decomposition of fractions with the same denominator, including mixed numbers and fractions greater than 1, using multiple representations. Limit fractions to denominators of 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 8, 10, 12, 20, 25, 50, and 100.
description_plain: Your child can break apart and put together fractions with the same bottom number, including mixed numbers, using pictures or models.

subject: Math
description: Generate equivalent fractions, including fractions greater than 1, using multiple representations. Limit fractions to denominators of 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 8, 10, 12, 20, 25, 50, and 100.
description_plain: Your child can create fractions that are equal to each other, including those bigger than one, using different ways to show them.

subject: Math
description: Compare decimal numbers to the hundredths using the benchmarks 0, 0.5, and 1.0, concrete area, and linear models. Use the symbols for is equal to (=), is less than (<), and/or is greater than (>).
description_plain: Your child can compare decimal numbers up to two decimal places using examples and symbols like =, <, and >.

subject: Math
description: Represent fractions with denominators of 10 and 100 in words, models, and decimal notations.
description_plain: Your child can show fractions with 10 or 100 as the bottom number using words, pictures, and decimals.

subject: Math
description: Represent and compare fractions in multiple ways using part-whole relationships.
description_plain: Your child can show and compare fractions in different ways by understanding parts of a whole.

subject: Math
description: Order whole numbers within 999,999 (no more than 3) in ascending or descending order and record the comparison(s) using symbols for is less than (<) and/or is greater than (>).Represent and compare fractions in multiple ways using part-whole relationships
description_plain: Your child can put whole numbers up to 999,999 in order from smallest to largest or largest to smallest and use < or > to show the comparison.

subject: Math
description: Estimate sums, differences, products, and quotients of multi-digit whole numbers, using rounding and place value to determine the reasonableness of real-world problem solutions. Write an equation for the estimate.
description_plain: Your child can make good guesses about adding, subtracting, multiplying, or dividing big numbers by rounding and explain their estimate with an equation.

subject: Math
description: Read and write whole numbers through the millions period (0 to 999,999,999) in word, standard, and equations in expanded form.
description_plain: Your child can read and write whole numbers up to 999,999,999 in words, standard form, and expanded form.

subject: Math
description: Represent and compare numbers using relationships within the base ten number system.
description_plain: Your child understands how to show and compare numbers using the base ten system.

subject: Math
description: Numerical Reasoning
description_plain: Numerical Reasoning

subject: Math
description: Classify quadrilaterals in a hierarchy based on their shared attributes.
description_plain: Your child can group four-sided shapes by their shared features in a logical order.

subject: Math
description: Classify triangles according to side length (isosceles, equilateral, scalene) and angle measure (acute, obtuse, right, equiangular).
description_plain: Your child can sort triangles by their side lengths and angle types.

subject: Math
description: Extend geometric reasoning to attributes of polygons and/or polyhedrons.
description_plain: Your child can use what they know about shapes to understand more about polygons and 3D shapes.

subject: Math
description: Convert customary units of length, weight, and liquid volume from a larger unit to a smaller unit, given direct comparisons of the two measurements and/or the unit equivalencies within a single system of measurement. Limit to inches, feet, yards, ounces, pounds, fluid ounces, cups, pints, quarts, and gallons when given unit equivalencies.
description_plain: Your child can change larger units of length, weight, or liquid into smaller units using common measurements like inches, feet, pounds, and gallons.

subject: Math
description: Measure weight in customary units and metric units to the nearest whole unit. Limit to ounces, pounds, grams, and kilograms.
description_plain: Your child can measure weight using both customary units like ounces and pounds, and metric units like grams and kilograms.

subject: Math
description: Measure length to the nearest quarter inch.
description_plain: Your child can measure length accurately to the nearest quarter inch.

subject: Math
description: Solve real-world situations involving addition and subtraction of time intervals within 60 minutes to find elapsed time, start time, or end time.
description_plain: Your child can solve problems about time by adding or subtracting minutes within an hour to find start, end, or elapsed time.

subject: Math
description: Calculate the value of a collection of coins and bills in real-world situations to determine whether there is enough money to make a purchase. Justify based on comparison of money amounts.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the total value of coins and bills to see if there is enough money to buy something and explain their answer.

subject: Math
description: Estimate and measure using units of length, liquid volume, weight, currency, and intervals of time.
description_plain: Your child can estimate and measure length, volume, weight, money, and time intervals.

subject: Math
description: Apply area formulas for rectangles to solve real-world situations. Use square units to label area measurements.
description_plain: Your child can use formulas to find the area of rectangles and label the answer with square units.

subject: Math
description: Apply perimeter formulas for rectangles to solve real-world situations including finding the perimeter, given the side lengths, and finding an unknown side length.
description_plain: Your child can use formulas to find the perimeter of rectangles and solve for missing side lengths.

subject: Math
description: Solve area and perimeter problems in real-world and mathematical situations.
description_plain: Your child can solve problems involving the area and perimeter of shapes in real life and math.

subject: Math
description: Measurement, Geometry, and Spatial Reasoning
description_plain: Your child learns about measuring, shapes, and understanding space.

subject: Math
description: Determine the possible outcomes of a simple event and record the probability as certain, possible, or impossible.
description_plain: Your child can find all possible results of a simple event and say if each is certain, possible, or impossible.

subject: Math
description: Represent the probability of simple events and determine possible outcomes.
description_plain: Your child can show the chance of simple events and figure out what might happen.

subject: Math
description: Solve one-step, real-world situations using whole number and fractional data represented in tables, scaled picture graphs, scaled bar graphs, or dot plots. Limit to like denominators of 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 8, and 10.
description_plain: Your child can solve one-step problems using whole numbers and fractions shown in tables and different types of graphs.

subject: Math
description: Collect and organize numerical and categorical data based on observations, investigations, surveys, and experiments using tables, scaled bar graphs, or dot plots. Use titles and labels. Scales to include whole numbers, halves, and fourths.
description_plain: Your child can collect and organize data from observations or surveys using tables and graphs with titles and labels.

subject: Math
description: Create questions, collect and analyze data, and communicate interpretations through multiple representations.
description_plain: Your child can ask questions, gather data, analyze it, and explain what it means using different ways to show information.

subject: Math
description: Data, Probability, and Statistical Reasoning
description_plain: Your child can collect, organize, and understand data to make predictions and decisions.

subject: Math
description: Identify and apply regularity in repeated reasoning to make generalizations.
description_plain: Your child can notice patterns in repeated math steps and use them to make general rules.

subject: Math
description: Structure & Patterns
description_plain: Structure & Patterns

subject: Math
description: Use critical thinking skills to reason both abstractly and quantitatively.
description_plain: Your child can think carefully to understand and solve math problems using both ideas and numbers.

subject: Math
description: Analyze & Justify
description_plain: Analyze & Justify

subject: Math
description: Demonstrate a deep and flexible conceptual understanding of mathematical ideas, operations, and relationships while making real-world connections.
description_plain: Your child understands math ideas well and can connect them to real-life situations.

subject: Math

description_plain: Connections

subject: Math
description: Explain ideas using precise and contextually appropriate mathematical language, tools, and models.
description_plain: Your child can explain math ideas clearly using the right words, tools, and pictures.

subject: Math
description: Representation & Communication
description_plain: Your child learns how to show and talk about math ideas.

subject: Math
description: Make sense of problems and persevere in solving them strategically.
description_plain: Your child can understand math problems and keep trying different ways to solve them.

subject: Math
description: Problem Solving
description_plain: Problem Solving

subject: Math
description: Mathematical Process Standards
description_plain: Your child learns important math thinking and problem-solving skills.

subject: Math
description: Apply all operations with rational numbers to solve problems in mathematical and real-world situations.
description_plain: Your child can use addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division with fractions and decimals to solve math and real-life problems.

subject: Math
description: Factor linear expressions with integer coefficients using the greatest common factor (GCF).
description_plain: Your child can break down simple algebra expressions by finding the biggest number that divides all parts.

subject: Math
description: Recognize that algebraic expressions may have a variety of equivalent forms and determine an appropriate form for a given real-world situation.
description_plain: Your child understands that algebra expressions can look different but mean the same, and can choose the best form for a real problem.

subject: Math
description: Identify linear expressions that are equivalent.
description_plain: Your child can tell when two algebra expressions are equal.

subject: Math
description: Simplify numerical expressions that include integer exponents using the laws of exponents: the Product of Powers, Quotient of Powers, Power of a Power, Power of a Product, Power of a Quotient, Zero Power, and Negative Exponent.
description_plain: Your child can simplify math expressions with powers, like multiplying or dividing numbers with exponents.

subject: Math
description: Apply mathematical patterns, properties, and algorithms to the set of rational numbers to find sums, differences, products, and quotients and to write equivalent expressions.
description_plain: Your child can use patterns and rules to add, subtract, multiply, divide, and rewrite fractions and decimals.

subject: Math
description: Use dimensional analysis to convert units between metric and customary systems.
description_plain: Your child can change measurements from metric to customary units and back.

subject: Math
description: Compute unit rates, including those involving complex fractions with like or different units.
description_plain: Your child can find unit rates, even when the numbers are fractions or use different units.

subject: Math
description: Write and evaluate expressions in one variable that model mathematical and real world situations.
description_plain: Your child can write and solve simple math expressions with one variable to explain real-life situations.

subject: Math
description: Write and solve multi-step equations and inequalities in one variable involving rational numbers in mathematical and real-world situations.
description_plain: Your child can write and solve multi-step math problems with one variable using fractions and decimals.

subject: Math
description: Write, simplify, and solve algebraic expressions, equations, and inequalities.
description_plain: Your child can write, simplify, and solve algebra expressions, equations, and inequalities.

subject: Math
description: Identify the constant of proportionality within proportional relationships.
description_plain: Your child can find the constant number that shows how two things change together in a proportional relationship.

subject: Math
description: Create a model with functions that address a proportional relationship in real world situations.
description_plain: Your child can create a math model to show how two things are proportional in real life.

subject: Math
description: Apply proportional reasoning to solve problems in mathematical and real-world situations involving ratios and percentages.
description_plain: Your child can use ratios and percentages to solve problems where quantities change in proportion.

subject: Math
description: Use tables, graphs, verbal descriptions, or equations to represent a function.
description_plain: Your child can use tables, graphs, words, or equations to show how one quantity depends on another.

subject: Math
description: Compare two rational numbers and write statements using is equal to (=), is not equal to (≠), is less than (<), is greater than (>), is greater than or equal to (≥), and/or is less than or equal to (≤) in mathematical and real-world situations.
description_plain: Your child can compare two fractions or decimals and use symbols like =, ≠, <, >, ≤, or ≥ to show their relationship.

subject: Math
description: Utilize rational numbers in mathematical and real-world situations.
description_plain: Your child can use fractions, decimals, and negative numbers to solve math and real-life problems.

subject: Math
description: Convert rational numbers into equivalent forms among fractions (including mixed numbers), decimals, and percentages. Exclude the conversion of repeating decimals to fractions.
description_plain: Your child can change fractions, decimals, and percentages into each other, except for repeating decimals to fractions.

subject: Math
description: Translate among multiple representations of rational numbers.
description_plain: Your child can switch between different ways to show fractions, decimals, and percentages.

subject: Math
description: Find distances between ordered pairs on the coordinate plane, limited to the same x-coordinate or the same y-coordinate.
description_plain: Your child can find the distance between two points on a graph when they share the same x-value or the same y-value.

subject: Math
description: Graph on the coordinate plane.
description_plain: Your child can plot points and draw graphs on a coordinate plane.

subject: Math
description: Write and solve equations to solve mathematical and real-world situations involving the relationships among angles formed by two intersecting lines. Limit to supplementary, complementary, vertical, and adjacent relationships.
description_plain: Your child can write and solve equations to find missing angle measures when two lines cross, using special angle relationships like supplementary, complementary, vertical, and adjacent angles.

subject: Math
description: Identify the relationships and measures among angles formed by two intersecting lines, given the measure of one angle. Limit to supplementary, complementary, vertical, and adjacent relationships.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the sizes and relationships of angles formed by two crossing lines when given one angle, focusing on supplementary, complementary, vertical, and adjacent angles.

description_plain: Your child can draw flat patterns (nets) that fold into 3D shapes.

subject: Math
description: Find the area of a triangle, square, rectangle, parallelogram, and trapezoid.
description_plain: Your child can find the area of triangles, squares, rectangles, parallelograms, and trapezoids.

subject: Math
description: Determine the measurements of geometric figures.
description_plain: Your child can measure sides and angles of different shapes.

subject: Math
description: Measurement, Geometry, and Spatial Reasoning
description_plain: Your child learns about measuring, shapes, and how objects fit in space.

subject: Math
description: Given the probability of an event, identify and calculate the complement of that event.
description_plain: Your child can find the chance that an event will not happen by using the given probability.

subject: Math
description: Find the probability of simple events in mathematical and real-world situations. Limit denominators to 2, 4, 5, 8, 10, 25, 50, and 100.
description_plain: Your child can find the chance of simple events happening, using fractions with certain denominators.

subject: Math
description: Given the probability of a random event, expressed as a number from 0 to 1, state the likelihood of the event occurring.
description_plain: Your child can explain how likely an event is to happen when given a number between 0 and 1.

subject: Math
description: Calculate and interpret probability.
description_plain: Your child can calculate and understand the chance of events happening.

subject: Math
description: Calculate and interpret the median, mode, range, interquartile range in mathematical and real-world situations.
description_plain: Your child can find and explain the middle value, most common value, range, and spread of data in math problems and everyday examples.

subject: Math
description: Use the shape of the graph to determine whether median or mode best describes the data set.
description_plain: Your child can look at a graph's shape and decide if the middle value or the most common value better shows the data.

subject: Math
description: Create box plots to represent numerical data sets in mathematical and real-world situations.
description_plain: Your child can make box plots to show sets of numbers from math and real-life situations.

subject: Math
description: Identify the sample size for a numerical set of data in mathematical and real-world situations.
description_plain: Your child can tell how many numbers are in a data set from math problems or real-life examples.

subject: Math
description: Analyze data sets to identify their statistical elements.
description_plain: Your child can study groups of data to find important parts like averages and ranges.

subject: Math
description: Data, Probability, and Statistical Reasoning
description_plain: Your child understands how to work with data, chance, and statistics.

subject: Math
description: Identify and apply regularity in repeated reasoning to make generalizations.
description_plain: Your child can notice patterns in repeated math steps and use them to make general rules.

subject: Math
description: Structure & Patterns
description_plain: Structure & Patterns

subject: Math
description: Use critical thinking skills to reason both abstractly and quantitatively.
description_plain: Your child can think carefully to understand and solve math problems using both ideas and numbers.

subject: Math
description: Analyze & Justify
description_plain: Analyze & Justify

subject: Math
description: Demonstrate a deep and flexible conceptual understanding of mathematical ideas, operations, and relationships while making real-world connections.
description_plain: Your child understands math ideas well and can connect them to real-life situations.

subject: Math
description: Connections
description_plain: Connections

subject: Math
description: Explain ideas using precise and contextually appropriate mathematical language, tools, and models.
description_plain: Your child can explain math ideas clearly using the right words, tools, and pictures.

subject: Math
description: Representation & Communication
description_plain: Your child learns how to show and talk about math ideas.

subject: Math
description: Make sense of problems and persevere in solving them strategically.
description_plain: Your child can understand math problems and keep trying different ways to solve them.

subject: Math
description: Problem Solving
description_plain: Problem Solving

subject: Math
description: Mathematical Process Standards
description_plain: Your child learns important math thinking and problem-solving skills.

subject: Math
description: Translate a two-step real-world situation into a numerical expression using parentheses as grouping symbols and evaluate the expression.
description_plain: Your child can change a two-step real-life problem into a math expression with parentheses and solve it.

subject: Math
description: Identify a rule that can describe the pattern from the data of a function table and write it as an expression.
description_plain: Your child can find a rule from a pattern in a table and write it as a math expression.

subject: Math
description: Determine the greatest common factor (GCF) of two numbers both less than or equal to 50 to simplify a fraction into its standard form.
description_plain: Your child can find the biggest number that divides two numbers (up to 50) to simplify fractions.

subject: Math
description: Determine the least common multiple (LCM) to find a common denominator. Limit denominators to 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 8, 10, 12, 20, 25, 50, and 100.
description_plain: Your child can find the smallest common multiple of certain numbers to help find common denominators.

subject: Math
description: Use reasoning to represent and solve algebraic and numerical situations.
description_plain: Your child can use thinking skills to show and solve math problems with numbers and simple algebra.

subject: Math
description: Interpret and represent division of a whole number dividend by a unit fraction divisor and a unit fraction dividend by a whole number divisor and apply to real-world situations. Limit denominators to 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 8, 10, and 12.
description_plain: Your child can understand and show division problems where whole numbers and simple fractions divide each other, and use these in real-life examples.

subject: Math
description: Use a strategy to multiply a fraction by a fraction or a fraction by a whole to include real-world situations. Limit denominators to 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 8, 10, and 12.
description_plain: Your child can use methods to multiply fractions by fractions or whole numbers, including real-life problems.

subject: Math
description: Use a strategy to compute sums and differences of fractions and mixed numbers with unlike denominators and justify the sum or difference to include real-world situations. Limit denominators to 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 8, 10, 12, 20, 25, 50, and 100.Use a strategy to multiply a fraction by a fraction or a fraction by a whole to include real-world situations. Limit denominators to 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 8, 10, and 12.
description_plain: Your child can add and subtract fractions and mixed numbers with different denominators and explain their answers, including real-life examples.

subject: Math
description: Use multiple representations to reason and solve problems involving operational properties of fractions.
description_plain: Your child can use different ways to think about and solve problems with fractions.

subject: Math
description: Use a strategy to multiply a one-digit whole number by a decimal to the hundredths and divide a decimal to the hundredths (dividend) by a one-digit whole number (divisor). Justify the calculation.
description_plain: Your child can multiply a one-digit number by a decimal (up to hundredths) and divide a decimal by a one-digit number, explaining their steps.

subject: Math
description: Use a strategy to compute sums and differences of decimal numbers to the hundredths.
description_plain: Your child can add and subtract decimal numbers up to the hundredths place using a method.

subject: Math
description: Use a strategy to compute the quotient of a multi-digit whole number dividend divided by a two-digit whole number divisor, with and without remainders, to include real-world situations. Limit the dividend to four digits.
description_plain: Your child can divide a whole number (up to four digits) by a two-digit number, with or without leftovers, and use this in real-life problems.

subject: Math
description: Use a strategy to compute the product of a two- or three-digit factor times a two-digit factor to include real-world situations.
description_plain: Your child can multiply two- or three-digit numbers by two-digit numbers and use this skill in real-life situations.

subject: Math
description: Use multiple representations to reason and solve problems involving operational properties of whole numbers and decimals.
description_plain: Your child can use different ways to think about and solve problems with whole numbers and decimals.

subject: Math
description: Patterns, Algebra, and Function Reasoning
description_plain: Your child can recognize and work with patterns and simple algebra ideas to solve problems.

subject: Math
description: Compare fractions and mixed numbers with like and unlike denominators of 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 8, 10, 12, 20, 25, and 100 using equivalence to create a common denominator. Use the symbols for is less than (<), is more than (>), or is equal to (=) to record the comparison.
description_plain: Your child can compare fractions and mixed numbers by finding common denominators and use <, >, or = to show which is bigger or if they are equal.

subject: Math
description: Represent and compare fractions in multiple ways.
description_plain: Your child can show fractions in different ways and compare them to understand their sizes.

subject: Math
description: Use patterns to explain the exponents when multiplying and dividing by powers of 10, not to exceed the thousandths place.
description_plain: Your child can use patterns to explain how multiplying or dividing by 10, 100, or 1000 changes a number’s decimal place.

subject: Math
description: Round decimal numbers up to 999 with decimals to the thousandths place to the nearest hundredth, tenth, or whole number.
description_plain: Your child can round decimal numbers up to 999 with decimals to the thousandths place to the nearest whole number, tenth, or hundredth.

subject: Math
description: Explain how the value of a digit in a multi-digit number changes if the digit moves one or more places to the left or right in the base ten system. Include decimals to the thousandths place.
description_plain: Your child understands how a digit’s value changes when it moves left or right in a number, including decimals to the thousandths place.

subject: Math
description: Read, write, and represent multi-digit numbers from 0 to 999 with decimals to the thousandths place. Use pictorial, word, standard, or expanded form with fraction or decimal notation.
description_plain: Your child can read, write, and show numbers from 0 to 999 with decimals to the thousandths place in words, pictures, or different number forms.

subject: Math
description: Represent and compare numbers using relationships within the base ten number system.
description_plain: Your child can show and compare numbers by understanding how the base ten system works.

subject: Math
description: Numerical Reasoning
description_plain: Numerical Reasoning

subject: Math
description: Represent mathematical and real-world situations by graphing, labeling, and interpreting points in the first quadrant of the coordinate plane.
description_plain: Your child can graph points on the coordinate plane’s first quadrant and explain what the points mean in math and real life.

subject: Math
description: Identify the origin, x-axis, and y-axis in the coordinate system. Write, plot, and label ordered pairs, including values in a function table, in the first quadrant of the coordinate plane.
description_plain: Your child can find and name the origin, x-axis, and y-axis, and plot points using ordered pairs in the first quadrant.

subject: Math
description: Graph on the coordinate plane.
description_plain: Your child can make graphs on the coordinate plane.

subject: Math
description: Estimate and measure lengths to the nearest eighth of an inch or nearest millimeter.
description_plain: Your child can estimate and measure lengths accurately to the nearest eighth of an inch or millimeter.

subject: Math
description: Given the unit equivalencies, convert within a single system of measurement from larger units to smaller units and smaller units to larger units for length, weight, liquid volume, and time. Use these conversions in solving real-world situations. Limit units to inches, feet, yards, ounces, pounds, fluid ounces, cups, pints, quarts, gallons, seconds, minutes, hours, milli-, centi-, kilo-, and.base units (grams, liters, meters)
description_plain: Your child can change measurements from bigger to smaller units and back (like feet to inches or minutes to seconds) and use these to solve real problems.

subject: Math
description: Convert within a given measurement system and measure length.
description_plain: Your child can convert measurements within one system and measure lengths.

subject: Math
description: Estimate and measure the volume of a right rectangular prism with whole-number side lengths by filling it with unit cubes.
description_plain: Your child can estimate and measure the volume of a box-shaped object by filling it with unit cubes.

subject: Math
description: Solve problems involving area and perimeter of composite figures by decomposing with rectangles.
description_plain: Your child can solve problems about area and perimeter by breaking shapes into rectangles.

subject: Math
description: Solve area, perimeter, and volume problems in real-world and mathematical situations.
description_plain: Your child can solve real and math problems involving area, perimeter, and volume.

subject: Math
description: Measurement, Geometry, and Spatial Reasoning
description_plain: Your child learns about measuring, shapes, and understanding space.

subject: Math
description: Represent the probability of a simple event as 0, a fraction, or 1. Limit fractions to denominators of 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 8, 10, 20, and 25.
description_plain: Your child can show the chance of a simple event happening as 0, 1, or a fraction with certain denominators.

subject: Math
description: Represent the probability of simple events and determine possible outcomes.
description_plain: Your child can find the chance of simple events and figure out all possible outcomes.

subject: Math
description: Analyze categorical and numerical data in graphical displays to make predictions or draw conclusions. Limit displays to tables, bar graphs, dot plots, line graphs, and circle graphs with scales of whole numbers, halves, fourths, and eighths.
description_plain: Your child can look at different types of graphs and tables with whole numbers and simple fractions to make guesses or conclusions about the data.

subject: Math
description: Solve two-step, real-world situations using whole number and fractional data represented in tables, line graphs, scaled bar graphs, or dot plots. Limit fractions to denominators of 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 8, 10, 12, 20, 25, 50, and 100.
description_plain: Your child can solve real-life problems that need two steps using whole numbers and common fractions shown in tables and graphs.

subject: Math
description: Describe data by determining the range and mode, including whole numbers, fractional data, and decimal data. Limit fractions to denominators of 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 8, and 10, and limit decimals to decimals through the hundredths place.
description_plain: Your child can describe data by finding the range and most common value using whole numbers, simple fractions, and decimals up to two decimal places.

subject: Math
description: Create questions, collect and analyze data, and communicate through multiple representations.
description_plain: Your child can ask questions, gather data, study it, and share what they find in different ways.

subject: Math
description: Data, Probability, and Statistical Reasoning
description_plain: Your child understands how to work with data, chance, and statistics.

subject: Math
description: Identify and apply regularity in repeated reasoning to make generalizations.
description_plain: Your child can notice patterns in repeated math steps and use them to make general rules.
